text: University: University of Central Florida | Overall Rating: 4.1 | Ratings: Reputation: 4, Location: 4, Opportunities: 4, Facilities: 4, Internet: 3, Food: 3, Clubs: 4, Social: 4, Happiness: 4, Safety: 4 | Review: UCF has a great infrastructure!

University: University of Central Florida | Overall Rating: 4.0 | Ratings: Reputation: 4, Location: 4, Opportunities: 4, Facilities: 4, Internet: 1, Food: 1, Clubs: 4, Social: 4, Happiness: 4, Safety: 4 | Review: Loved my time at UCF. 63 south is bad and the internet can be quite poor basically anywhere on campus though.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: A name that will always be remembered in the UCF comp sci department, and not in a good way. His office smelled so bad and he was really weird towards his female TA. I can confirm that the rumors were true and that we were all uncomfortable. Meade marked a stain on UCF's name and I hope it doesn't happen again.
Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Audit/No Grade | Review: Yo! Is That The Hello Kitty Man?

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Drop/Withdrawal | Review: Took this class during undergrad for CS1. He often started lectures 15 minutes early. During a semester when a hurricane devastated the area and some students had flooded apartments or totaled cars, getting extensions or makeups felt very difficult. His interactions with one of the TAs also made many students uncomfortable.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: The best professor I have had in my undergrad. This class was difficult, which is needed to burn these concepts in your mind. This is the only class that I didn't just "memorize, test, forget, repeat". I remember everything in this class, and Dr. Meade is an amazing professor. Expect to do work, which is needed to learn concepts.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3223C | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: B | Review: Professor was average (if not subpar) for the time he did actually teach; however, he abruptly left the university without any notice halfway through the semester, leaving students at the hands of another professor.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: I enjoyed this class a lot. If you've heard that Meade's classes are fast-paced, you'd be correct, but it wasn't so fast that it was stressful. There are easy quizzes and very complicated homework assignments weekly. He goes over exams in detail. He has massive curves, grade drops and an extra credit assignment. He wants you to succeed ask for help

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: B+ | Review: CS1 is a hard course, if you're a computer science major you need to know the topics covered in this course really well so you can pass the foundation exam. Dr. Meade's class is really hard, not gonna lie, but as long as you ask for help, office hours or friends, you should be fine. His coding assignments are rough but you will learn the topics

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: B+ | Review: He is proud of the fact that his test questions and homework assignments are written to be confusing. He completely ignored my emails. During his office hours, he pawned me off on the unhelpful TA and during the TAs office hours, the TA told me to youtube my questions. His lectures assume a competency not required by the syllabus. Never again!

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: this class is impossibly hard. he talks at the speed of light and expects topics to be known that werent taught in previous courses(not rlly his fault) for the "syllabus"/fin aid assignment you WILL fail, he lets you make it up till the final. gives extra credit in the form of comm serv hrs. dont go to lectures = you will fail the class. ~50% = C

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3223C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: C | Review: although cs1 is already a difficult course as it is, Meade makes it more complicated than it already is. The assignments are long and tedious, tests are pretty difficult as well. C line was a 46 this past semester. Go to office hours often if you want to pass lol

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: B+ | Review: Not easy if you had a rough intro to c but it IS doable. + Him and his TAs are really helpful in office hours
Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: A | Review: Super funny and gives a massive curve at the end of the class:)

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: the negatives most likely fell behind in intro to C at some point or didnt fully understand a concept before entering this class. if you come into this class with a good understanding and you follow along, you can get an A easily. hes passionate about what he teaches and he knows what hes doing.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: I really appreciate the way that Dr. Meade teaches this course.  As someone who went into this class with years of programming experience, I was still challenged and found his lectures interesting and engaging.  Despite the challenge, it is a very passable course due the generous curve.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: CS1 is supposed to be hard, it is the most difficult CS class; dont know what others are on about. Anyone with basic C syntax knowledge can pass but just make sure to work hard for it. If you want an easy A, dont take him. But if youre prepared to dedicate most of your time practicing and enjoy assignments that make you think then take prof Meade.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: B | Review: Meade's class is hard, but CS1 is a difficult subject by itself... If you only rely on his lectures and don't code and learn in your free time, then don't blame him for your lack of effort. His assignments/tests prepares you for the FE. Failing with the huge curve means you're not prepared.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: Dr. Meade has plenty of resources (office hours, typed notes, his replit). Plus there are more resources online! So dont fully rely on him to hold your hand throughout CS1. His lectures can get disorganized, but he knows his stuff, so dont be afraid to go to office hours! (12 hours of studying is expected weekly in CS1 for EVERY class, get ready!)

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: bring back the old hair PLEASE! in all seriousness, meade is very passionate about teaching computer science, and it shows through office hours, lectures, and really you can just tell. the assignments are very difficult, but it definitely help me be a better programmer. i knew absolutely nothing, but just put alot of time n effort, and you'll pass.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: Probably the worst CS1 professor available. You will end up having to teach yourself nearly all the concepts. I recommend avoiding unless you are highly proficient in data structures and algorithms before even starting the class.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: B | Review: Good at teaching, very knowledgeable, and hes pretty funny but the 7 homeworks and 2 tests + final are VERY difficult. Take him if you're a CS major that wants to be slightly more prepared for the FE, otherwise just avoid him. Massive curve at the end of the semester based on class average so that's nice, usually ends up in the low 50's for a C.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: Not sure what others are going on about 40s on the test. Test 1 avg was a 51.5 and Test 2 avg was a 53.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: People hate his lectures, and idk why. Sure he draws his slides on paper and uses VIM for code, but he's really passionate about what he teaches AND knows how to teach it well. He uses FndtnExam format for tests and code challenges for assignments, but his lectures are the best ones I've attended. Maybe it's cause I know CS already. 10/10 for pros?

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: He is a very intelligent and funny guy, but only take him if data structures and algorithms come naturally to you. Assignments were extremely complicated and long, exam reviews were vague and not very helpful (but you do get a cheat sheet). If you miss a day of class and do not have someone to help you, I'm sorry. TAs had a hard time helping us out

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: This class took years off my life. Professor spoke to students with the assumption they had the same level of understanding on topics as him. Test averages were unsurprisingly bad, somewhere around 40. Not really any resources besides the code he does in class which he speeds through with little explanation. Assignments were hard and long too.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: A funny, smart professor who does not want you to succeed. No resources to access outside of class, the labs are unimportant and usually aren't explained, and notes posted are unreadable without context (lectures are not recorded like Ahmed's). Told students to withdraw if they did badly on the first test.. which had an average of a ~48.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3223H | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: + Informative lectures
+ Light homework load with engaging problems
+ Notes allowed on exams

- Fast-paced, disorganized lectures (still covered important info)
- Some homework/exam problems were too difficult

I wouldn't recommend this professor for beginners, though, due to the lecture pace and complexity.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: B+ | Review: CS1 is a hard class, I get that. However, this Professor made the class a lot harder than it had to be. The assignments take up a lot of time and most of the time you are not going to get a 100. He does give a cheat sheet on the exams, but the exams are very hard. Getting 40s and 50s on the exams are pretty good. There is a big curve!!

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: C | Review: There were a lot of assignments, but all the test cases are not provided so you'll never know what you're losing points for. The labs don't help much if at all for the weekly quizzes. The exams had very low averages, there is tons of partial credit on them. Exams are allowed a one-page front and back cheat sheet. For the final, it was two.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COT3100C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: The most incompetent and unprofessional professor. Didn't post grades until after the final and doesn't answer emails. I had to email him 4 times + DM one of his TAs for a response. The people who passed are thanks to James the TA. The people who took him online had it way easier because his exams were open notes. He'll never round up if u need it.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COT3100C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: D | Review: This man does not deserve a job teaching anything. The fact that more than half of the graded content did not get started to get posted until 3-5 days AFTER the final is just an unhealthy way of learning when you cannot even gauge how you are doing in class. The people who passed also give all the credit to the TAs, not Meade. Doesn't answer emails

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COT3100C | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: Professor Meade's class is difficult. Get ready to work. He usually starts lectures early and has very scattered hand-written notes, but his knowledge of the material is unquestionable. He usually grades things very slowly and you will probably not know where you stand in the class even leading up to the final. You will learn a lot if you work hard

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: B | Review: Meade is an extremely intelligent professor but lacks in online course materials for studying. HIs exams are formatted exactly like the Foundation Exam which is great for preparing you. Overall, class had a huge curve at the end which really saved a lot of us, as long as you stay at around a 50% you should be passing.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: B | Review: Professor Meade is great, but he isn't for everyone. If you understand stuff quick and don't like when a professor explains every little obvious detail, he is your guy. He isn't the most organized person, but I was fine with that. The class is hard, regardless who you take, you gotta study a lot and do the coding hw which isn't always easy.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: C | Review: This would be my second time taking this class and I would say that he is a very fair professor. He gives a generous curve due to the class being difficult and is a very intelligent person who knows what he is talking about. But make sure that you have a good knowledge about how C works, and review data structures and you should be good.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COT3100C | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: B | Review: Meade is a professor who clearly does not care about his students at all. He gets carried through this class by his TA, James who is always available and helpful to students. He starts lectures early, assumes the students know the material before hand, and his examples are hard to follow along through proofs. Take Goatber for discrete if you can.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: A | Review: Dr Meade cares about his students. He was always open to answering questions even outside his designated office hours. He will make sure you understand and take time to work out problems with you. This class was challenging due to the amount of content covered in such a short timespan, but I would not have gotten an A without his guidance.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COT3100C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: F | Review: Professor Meade is really smart, but he doesn't know how to teach this class. His lectures are hard to understand, he take to long to submit grades and notes taken in class, just try not to take this professor if you want to pass the class

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: F | Review: He does not read his emails and does not teach you anything !
You have to go through TA to get your best of help and I didnt learn anything on his lectures as if he tried to say I asked too many questions and said its not his job to give all the help he can .

ITS 50/50 OF PASSING and he does curve passing grade is 69-75.
Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COT3100C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: C+ | Review: go to lecture, he makes the class harder than it actually is tbh. dont take him.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: If you are serious about wanting to learn computer science, Meade is genuinely a great professor. His lectures are great, albeit fast paced, and he adds a lot of nuance. His assignments are difficult, but they will will make you a better programmer. He is also very accessible outside of class and his office hours are essentially mini lectures.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: Extremely fun and easy class as long as you know the basics, eases you into concepts for future classes. He's a very fun professor and while lectures aren't required if you know the subject already, its always fun. He is always willing to help on stuff after class even helps clarify stuff on tests. His grading curve is extremely generous.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3330 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: His lectures are a whirlwind of info. Very energetic very entertaining. His quizzes are terrible with lots of 'gotcha' questions that test unimportant things. Overall, he knows what he's doing and I learned a lot. Assignments are pretty difficult. Start early.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COT3100C | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: Meade taught this class well. Homeworks had an appropriate difficulty, but exams were hard. But, he curves the final grades at the end so the challenge is nice. If you don't show up to lecture you won't pass this class because the material can be tough. But it was a fun class and don't be afraid to take Meade.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COT3100C | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: A | Review: Meade is a very smart person, however I don't think they convey that knowledge very well. I had a hard time following a lot in their lectures. Their lectures can be good, but sometimes they go off on tangents and they are laid back enough to where you can get lost quickly. The labs were great practice, but you're gonna have to study a lot.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: Okay professor to be honest. I feel like the class will be difficult no matter the professor but I feel like in some form or other Dr Meade could have done it better after learning the content myself.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: B | Review: Smart dude, sometimes misses the mark on how to communicate ideas we're being taught. Office hours are pretty helpful but can get full. Tests challenge your critical thinking instead of echoing practice questions so make sure you actually understand topics. This class is passable but you have to go above and beyond for an A
Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: B+ | Review: Travis Meade is the goat. End of story.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COT3100C | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: I realize I am in the minority here, but Meade is a perfectly fine lecturer. He goes out of his way to answer as many questions as he can, there is a generous curve at the end of the course, and the tests were VERY similar to the homework questions. Because it was online, we could use our notes, which helped a lot. Overall a worthwhile class.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COT3100C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: Professor Travis Meade is a paragon of academic excellence, a beacon of knowledge in the realm of discrete structures. His genius is not confined to his profound understanding of the subject matter, but extends to his unique pedagogical approach that inspires students to transcend their academic boundaries.
Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502H | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: B | Review: Go to Office Hours!!!

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COT3100C | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: Professor Meade is a highly knowledgable and interesting professor, but he definitely assumes you know a fair bit of the content within the class before lectures. He's great in office hours, but his lecture style is very difficult to follow because it seems as though he is talking to someone who already thoroughly understands the topics.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: B | Review: COP3502 is hard. While I don't believe Prof. Meade makes it anymore difficult, I don't believe he makes it easier necessarily. If you go the lectures and attempt to the homework (they are hard, at least for me), I believe you'll be find. Study outside of class and practice for the exams as they matter. Decent curve (61 was a low B) and drops 2 HWs.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: B | Review: Meade is a good professor however the class is very difficult. He will keep you on the edge of your seat the entire semester with everyone failing the class and do a massive curve on the final grade. TONS of partial credit on exams so try to answer everything. Assignments are pretty hard so don't wait to do them. He dropped 2 labs and 2 assignments

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: B | Review: Professor Meade's CS1 class is incredibly difficult, but if you go to office hours to get help with the assignments you will get all the help you need.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: B | Review: Dr.Meade is given the tough task of teaching CS1 and preparing you for the FE, if you pass his class you will be able to pass the FE. Test are hard, but fair and if you understand how to do the homework youll be alright. Would recommend not using an IDE, go to office hours (theyre really good), and ask questions.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3223C | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: B | Review: Meade moves very quickly in lectures, so if you don't take notes well, or if you miss a class, you will fall behind incredibly quickly. Homework assignments are quite difficult and weighted extremely heavily, but if you go to office hours (which there are plenty of) you'll be fine. You need to be willing to put in a LOT of work to do well.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: C | Review: He isnt a bad teacher, im sure hes a good guy outside of class, but CS1 is no stroll in the park either. Be ready to study outside of class and work hard. Homeworks were hard and required lots of time. Test were also hard. as a tip, get familiar with runtimes, memory allocation, AVL trees, search algos and for exams, look at past foudnations exams
Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: B | Review: Very hard class and exam, but he try to help us. Good for our foundation exam.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: *If you want to slack off then Meade is NOT for you.* 
Meade is one of the most caring professors in the Computer Science department and it's not even close. Dr.Meade will start his office hours early/run them late just to help students. Use him as a resource and you WILL pass. Curve is VERY generous. Assignments will make you a better programmer.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: A | Review: I had a terrible experience with this professor. I took this class towards the end of my track, and in all my years of college I have never seen this amount of purposely confusing and time consuming homework assignments. Even though I will most likely end up with an A, its at what cost? This class did not need to be this hard.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: CS1 is a hard class so you have to cut Meade some slack. He tries to teach but he's just not good at it. If you struggled in COP3223, you will struggle in this class as he thinks you know everything already. Test are extremely hard and nothing like the practice test he gives and the homework is challenging. Only good thing about him is the curve

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: If you are a nerd programmer after taking Intro to C Programming, you shouldn't have much of a problem with Meade. I wouldn't recommend him if you didn't do as well in that class. Just make sure that when you complete his homework assignments that you apply enough efficiency in runtime as there is no partial credit available for the test cases.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: He makes the homework much harder than lectures so you don't know what you're doing. The homeworks are graded by test case input and outputs but he only gives you three of the test cases but grades by ten of them. So you think your code is perfect if you get three of them right but only end up with 30% of the test case part of the grade.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: C | Review: I don't think that any other professor would be much better. That being said don't expect to get much from the lectures but there are plenty of other resources like office hours. Just make sure you actually try and you will be fine. Having a bad professor is no guarantee of failure and having a good professor is no guarantee of success.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: A | Review: He graded half the course in the last week of the semester, wouldn't answer emails if you had to dispute something. He basically would always call the class dumb and he would make fun of us for trying to learn? I still got an A, but at what cost? Stay away from shmeade bean.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: B | Review: The best part of taking Dr. Meade is the generous curve he gives at the end of the course. The assignments are difficult and math heavy, but his class will prepare you for the FE. You're allowed one page front and back of notes on the exams, and he takes attendance for the labs. He's a funny guy, helpful in office hours and you WILL learn or fail.
Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: I would rather throw myself into a fiery volcano than take another lecture by this guy

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: B- | Review: Every 1-2 weeks there is a coding project, objectionably tough.
3 exams, 1 sheet of your notes.
Grades at end of course based of class grade.
Tough class, the professor doesn't mess around, you have to keep up.
Meade's job is to weed out the major, he does get you more ready for the foundation exam.
Attending lectures is vital for 90% of students.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: Math skills need to be strong, be strong with intro to C. Meade speaks English natively (yay). I  recorded lectures audio, used iPhone camera to capture code. He speaks fast, ton of material!  Plan to use tutors all the time.I spent 20 hours a week on class, took solo. Get ready for crappy grades exams are professional level. Curves based on class.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: Whether it is during office hours or after class if you as him a question or to further explain a concept he will go beyond to help you understand until you have no remaining questions.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: A | Review: This man will get you up to speed for CS1. He is extremely intelligent and is a great teacher, as noted; however, he is not the best when it comes to organization. He extends homework frequently to support the student and curves the class graciously (77 was an A). First few assignments will be tough but if you start early it shouldn't be a problem.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: His assignments very hard and unless you're very experienced with coding you will likely need to go to office hours. His tests where honestly not too bad. He's super knowledgeable and explains topics well however he seems to rush though the coding tutorials. Super nice guy though but id recommend being knowing your basics well before choosing him.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: B+ | Review: This class is difficult by nature, and Meade can be disorganized (notes not posted online and hard to contact via email). But the grading was super fair and the final grade line is dependent on how the class did which is great. Lecture attendance not required but lab attendance is graded

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: The class wasn't the easiest, but if you attend lectures and start assignments early you shouldn't have any problems. He does take forever to grade and is hard to reach outside of class, but lectures provide you with everything you need to pass the class. Plus you are allowed a sheet of notes for exams.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: B | Review: Meade is knowledgable, go to his office hours, take advantage of the TA/ULA/GTA hours to help understand the assignments. Where he falls short is not recording his lectures, not followable notes if you miss a lecture, doesn't post the in class code till a week after or even longer, not quick replies to emails(go to office hours to ask). Doable.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: Dr. Meade gives competitive programming problems as assignments. However, he also describes how to implement the solution. His tests were no harder than an AP CS A exam. He's also funny and cute. objectively.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: I really like Professor Meade, he is funny and extremely helpful in his office hours he also is quite generous when it comes to drops of assignments. He is a slow grader and he doesnt respond to emails that often but that comes with the class sizes and the nature of the class. Very generous grading scale shift at the end of the course.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: B | Review: Dr. Meade is easily the hottest professor in the CS department. He's smart, funny in lectures and seems like a genuinely kind person.
He teaches courses that are intense and meant to weed people out. He does generously curve at the end of the class though and if you go to office hours he won't give up on trying to explain the concepts.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: B | Review: By design CS1 is a hard topic. My first time taking this course was with Guha and it made me realize i knew nothing. With Meade it was a day and night difference, the lectures are well explained and he shows you the many ways of writing code, while also making you laugh along the way. Only negative is he starts his lectures like 15-20 minutes early

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: B | Review: Do not listen to negative feedback. Dr. Meade is an awesome professor loved by his students, he's not the best lecturer out there but you will definitely learn from his course. If you want to pass this class and learn, you have to take Dr meade. He lowers the grading scale based on the performance of the students unlike most of the professors

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: I've taken meade for two classes. He is a bit disorganized and it takes a while to graded assignments. honestly Dr. meade is one of my favorite professors. You may have to go out of your way to get extra help, but it is worth it. I have no prior C knowledge, he sat down and helped me and guided me to understanding. He's also hilarious

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: Meade knows what he's teaching but isn't always the best at teaching it. He is a bit unorganized too. Homeworks are challenging and so are tests, but they are fair. Be ready to go over content on your own and attend office hours if you have any problems.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: A | Review: Very hard class, but also not too difficult to pass or get an A in. The homework assignments will have you doing mental gymnastics for hours, so go to TA office hours, very helpful. 3 exams worth ~60% of grade, but he lets you have a note sheet during them. No curves on tests, but the final grade curve is helpful. Also, have 100% focus in lectures!

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3223C | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: Assignment instructions were a bit confusing (vague and unspecific), even though I already knew C. Doesn't use slides or anything. Has some good jokes, but definitely not the best teacher. Was unaccommodating when I had to miss an exam due to an extenuating circumstance.

I can't really complain too much.... curved the overall grade by 40 points.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: Meade was pretty much inaccessible outside of class except office hours. He took lightyears to grade assignments and test so no one knows their exact grade before the final. He did make the class harder so CS majors will be more prepared for the foundation exam. If you are an IT major or don't have to take the FE I recommend choosing someone else.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: There are 3/9 homework grades and 1/2 exam grades posted. No clue what I have in the class and my final is today. He is completely unreachable outside of class and skips his office hours sometimes. I have utterly no idea how he still has a job. This is the single worst professor I've ever had and it's not close. He couldn't care less if you fail.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: D | Review: NEVER TAKE THIS PROFESSOR!!! His lectures are incredibly unhelpful and will NEVER input a grade or respond to your emails. His tests are comically impossible his assignments are so overcomplicated the hardest part is just understanding what you're even supposed to do and his "hints" quite literally exist to throw you off course. Just don't do it.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COT3100C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: This professor is awful. If it weren't for the sheer amount of overtime the TA puts in for this class, most if not all the students would be failing. He doesn't respond to emails, doesn't show up to his own office hours half the time, doesn't know how to communicate the thing he is trying to teach, and sometimes won't even show up to class.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Drop/Withdrawal | Review: Would be an amazing stand up comedian, do not recommend if you're taking Comp Sci as a prerequisite for a major outside of Computer Science.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: A- | Review: Hardest class I have taken so far. His homeworks are extremely hard, was so difficult that the passing grade was about a 45. If you take this guy the only way to do well is to go to office hours. If you want to pass foundations exam this is your guy who will prepare you, hes the hardest beware. I really enjoyed the suffering at times, TYSM travis.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: You have no gage on how well your doing as it takes him over a month to grade anything. He does not provide any meaningful online resources and refuses to record lectures on zoom. Which would be fine if his lectures were also not bad. He starts early meaning if you are ON TIME to class your already missing information.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COT3100C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Do not take him. His exams are in person, under 10 questions on each one. It's the last week of classes and my homework 2 has yet to be graded. He doesn't answer his emails either, but he drops a couple hws, quizzes, and lab attendance. Lectures are not organized and he starts 10-15 minutes before class starts.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: Incomplete | Review: I believe Meade is an over-hated professor, but there is a reason he gets the hate he does. Grading takes forever and he will not respond to emails. It can be difficult to test your program with the limited test cases. Overall though I think this class is a great way to learn the content you need to.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: While Prof. Meade is very talented at coding and VERY intelligent, his lectures are all over the place and finds himself frequently sidetracked by other topics. He brings out advanced concepts during lecture that just confuse you and leave you lost. The notes/power points he uploads are barren and the assignments + tests are VERY difficult.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Drop/Withdrawal | Review: Although he is funny and charismatic in his lectures, the design of his course was unnecessarily grueling and difficult. The TAs were swamped trying to help our class with unnecessarily difficult homework and exams. This class is supposed to be an easier one, but not with Meade.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: B+ | Review: Honestly I was scared of taking this guy when I was looking at the reviews. But he wasn't bad at all. would have gave him a 5 if he didn't take so long to grade stuff.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Drop/Withdrawal | Review: A very disorganized professor. The lectures and HW assignments are confusing, messy, and overcomplicated. Do not recommend him-- unless you have a very strong programming foundation already, it is extremely difficult to learn the fundamentals from him. There are no modules on Webcourses either, just a collection of files that are randomly updated.
Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Incomplete | Review: Please do not take this class with this professor.
Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Incomplete | Review: Do not take discrete with him.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: His class is tough. His homeworks are time-consuming, but if you show up to office hours, he will bend over backward to help you. Even if you cant make his regular office hours he will do his best to work a time out to help you. If you put in the effort you will learn. You probably won't get a great grade on most assignments but he curves a lot.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: All the reviews online are true.  Stay away:  he starts classes early without notice.does not teach and assumes you learn in your own.  But he is paranoid people will use unauthorized sources to help them learn.  Like the internet

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Drop/Withdrawal | Review: This is a very tough class for someone who doesn't have all the knowledge in the world about programming. Would recommend you stay away from this prof

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Incomplete | Review: Only take this professor if you have an extensive programming background because he's going to explain everything like you're a senior developer with 10 years of experience. Lectures are extremely unorganized and somehow both start 10 minutes early and finish 10 minutes late.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Drop/Withdrawal | Review: After withdrawing from this course, I am now considering changing majors. I will probably be mentioning my struggles from this class at some point with my therapist

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COT3100C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Really awesome professor! Lectures are fun, engaging, and overflowing with so much valuable information. Professor Meade really keeps your attention and makes his topics clear and memorable. Definitely one of my favorite classes ever.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3223C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: Honestly one of the nicest and funniest professors I've had in a long time. The class can be hard for students who just want to take it and have no programming background, yes, but for any competent programmer, it's simple. Only have one or 2 assignments a week, gives great feedback, and even lets you bring notes to tests.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3223C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: A | Review: Dr. Travis is an amazing professor who cares about his students. He puts in more time and effort than he has to. His assignments and tests are hard but they prepared me for CS 1. He wants you to actually understand the material. He helped me with homework from another class, I can't emphasize more how Awesome this guy is. Take his class, work hard!

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COT3100C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: A | Review: This site won't let me click on more qualities that I believe wholeheartedly are ingrained in this dudes being. Funny funny man teach muy dificil clase. He is inspiring and melodramatic. He is very attentive during office hours and willing to help whenever. This enigma of a soul plagues my mind. Lots of love from Boston<3

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3223C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: I took this professor last semester and i miss him, his class was straight forward and if you asked a question by god he'd do everything to make you understand. He is genuinely really smart and when he isn't sure about something he tries it right in-front of the class instead of just saying idk. His humor made the class a hella lot more inviting

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: B | Review: Fast paced class that wasn't easy by any means, but going to office hours made a world of difference in understanding the content of the class. My main critiques were on the difficulty of the exams and the fact that the curve wasn't disclosed until after the final. Lectures were interesting and prof was incredibly helpful outside of lecture hours.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COT3100C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Drop/Withdrawal | Review: I put hours into a homework and Meade gave me a zero because it wasn't typed. His work is messy and he constantly tries to make jokes instead of teaching. If you get caught trying to get a tutor to help you with the class, YOU GET REPORTED FOR CHEATING. Overall this professor is nothing short of terrible. Do not take him if you want to pass.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COT3100C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: Drop/Withdrawal | Review: Travis meade is not a great professor. Apparently, he doesn't care if student can actually understand the materials before moving onto the next lessons. His teaching is very messy and extremely hard to follow, most of the times nobody in the class can understand or follow him. Is a very challenging class but with him is impossible.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3223C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: Meade is a funny charismatic guy. 7 assignments, 2 midterms, and a final. Lab had a quiz each week. Overall, pretty challenging in terms of problems he asks you to do. However, he is very accessible and helpful through office hours if you have any questions whatsoever. He is definitely not for non-CS majors, but if you like the challenge go for it.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3223C | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: You have to work to pass this class. 7-8 assignments are hard, but camp in his office hours & he's very helpful. Won't pass quizzes w/o labs. Tests are difficult, in person, handwritten, but generous with partial credit. He prepares you for CS1, so take him if you need more CS classes, otherwise take an easier prof. Curves final score distribution.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: B | Review: Prof Meade seems like a great guy. Very knowledgeable in C and in related subjects. However, his class has very poor structure. Oftentimes felt lost and thought he would overcomplicate assignments. You'll have to rely on outside materials to understand what is being taught. Teaching style is poor. Doesn't grade till end of ses, big curve at end

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COT3100C | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: He's a genius and you can tell that he's passionate about what he teaches. But, he doesn't do a very good job of conveying that. I feel like he overcomplicates things way too much. He is very disorganized and his teaching style reflects that. Funny, but goes off-topic sometimes.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3223C | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: C | Review: This is supposed to be an intro class and we basically skimmed over all the intro stuff and focused a majority of the semester on hard stuff. One homework assignment with 3 problems due every 2 weeks, but the problems are hard as hell considering I didn't learn much in the lectures. Only take if you have a coding background, otherwise stay away.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Do not take this professor or you'll have nothing but problems. His canvas was completely empty except for the syllabus. Assignments have do not have enough instructions, and if you don't follow the formatting he wants then you will be given a 0 with no chance of fixing that grade. Do not expect to be guaranteed recorded lectures either.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COT3100H | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: This class made me want to invent time travel just so I can find the first caveman to start thinking about numbers and kick him in the brain. - Meade assumes every one of his students loves math just as much as him, which is impossible, because he was a math major before comp sci. If you have any option to not take him, save yourself.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COT3100C | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: B | Review: Very charismatic and teaches the material pretty well. The lectures are where I got most of my information from, but there isn't an abundance of study material. It's a tricky class to teach and I've heard people complain about him, but I think it's mostly overblown. He really made the subject interesting to learn and he taught it well.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: Many people complaining here that his class is hard, which is true. BUT he teaches major early weedout courses: CS1, Discrete, etc. They are a wake-up call for people who think CS will be an easy major. If you want to succeed, you will have to work for it and take your education into your own hands. Believe me, it only gets harder from here on out.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Drop/Withdrawal | Review: Pros: He's funny. Cons: doesn't really explain concepts and gets off track easily. Homeworks and Tests are extremely hard. Lab quizzes should be an easy A but they are not. Usually gives two weeks for homework but I recommend you work in a group to try to not fail his class.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COT3100C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Dr. Meade is incredibly tough professor and seems to expect the student to know the information already. He will go over regular problems during class, but then on the exams and homeworks will pick the hardest ones for you to do. He also has admitted that he makes exams too difficult. Also is slow grader with a horrible scale so it gets annoying.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502H | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: Dr. Meade is an excellent professor, and he has been one of my favorite professors ever. He goes through a lot of content for the lectures, but if you're ever confused or need help on the homework, his office hours are a great help! His assignments/tests aren't the easiest, but they definitely made me prepared for the FE exam (I got a 90+ on it).

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502H | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: B | Review: Brilliant, extremely likable, super genuine, very clear grading criteria (especially homework), regular office hours that were extremely helpful, taught the information extraordinarily well. The class was super super difficult, and this honors version is way harder than the normal class, but take this teacher regardless if you're gonna work hard.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: He's not as bad as people make him out to be. The man tries his best. The class is just really hard. Also, the assignments get graded months after they're due, making feedback hard to come by. Definitely go to SI if you're having trouble with some concepts. P.S. thanks Meade for joining our Zoom study group at 11pm to answer our questions

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: B+ | Review: Dr. Meade has a great personality and cares about his students, but he has room to improve. He does well enough in lectures and is helpful in office hours, but the projects need to have clearer instructions. You do need to take good notes in this class, especially if you're a computer science major as the Foundation Exam right after.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: Meade made me chuckle almost every lecture, very friendly and an incredibly lenient grading scale. His assignments are either incredibly easy, or the most infuriating thing possible. 1/3 of our assignments were dropped and almost the same amount were dropped of labs. I've heard his teaching isn't for everyone but I had no difficulty with him.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: B | Review: Dr. Meade is a cool guy but not the best teacher, especially if you don't know what you're doing. CS dude bros will immediately bond with him and proceed to derail him for the rest of the semester. Unless you plan to rewatch lectures at .75x speed (fast talker) and find supplementary material yourself all semester long, pick a different professor.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: A | Review: Dr. Meade is the smartest professor I have ever had. He has an answer to any question you ask him about Computer Science. The benefit of taking Meade is being challenged like you never have before in a college class. If you are not ready to make his class your full-time job, you will not get an A or learn the material on a level he expects you to.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: A | Review: Hardest class Ive ever taken. Only passed because I went to EVERY office hour and SI session. You wont learn from Meade, youll learn from the SI leader. Office hours were essential to understanding the 8 assignments, 2 of which were dropped. Very unresponsive to emails, and very slow at grading. Curve was big though, 75+ was an A.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: A | Review: As just a math major, after taking intro to C I thought Comp sci would be a breeze. I was wrong. Meade teaches at a very high level, with the assignments being very in depth (6-8 hours each). Going to lectures and reviewing the logic of concepts allowed me to do well on the exams eventually earning an A. Its not impossible, but be prepared to work.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: Great professor, was very scared at first but he has a very big curve at the end. He really cares for his students and will spend several extra hours a week just to help students out and make sure they know the material.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: Best professor I've ever had. Meade makes me happy to be a part of Computer Science. People think this class is hard and that Meade is a hard teacher but that just because they aren't willing to put in the work for the class. All you need to do to pass the class with an A is listen in lectures and work hard on the problems given. 5/5 best teacher.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: I was luckily able to pass but only because of our peer tutor. You might as well skip class after you get checked in for attendance cuz you wont learn anything from the professor. First week perfectly describes this class and thats getting taught something then trying your hardest to code an assignment without having the knowledge to

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: Travis Meade is an extremely smart man. He is well versed in his programming abilities. Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Incomplete | Review: CS1 is objectively a very difficult class but he does best that he can with what little time he had with Covid online classes. Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: B | Review: Pros: Office Hours + time after class really informative and answers questions well, actual lectures good both in concepts and code, Curves based on the class Cons: Powerpoint slides are bad, assignments are sometimes not worded clearly, can get distracted by student comments Notes: Assignment difficulty varies, with some being harder than others

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Pros: Will stay after class to help kids Office hours are helpful Due dates are known End of class curve Cares about his students Cons: Distracted easily by zoom chat, lectures will run over Assignment instructions are confusing, not proofread Grading time Email response time I like meade, his class is very hard, so don't slack. Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: He expects you to fail the first assignment, and when you do you'll be unmotivated to complete the next one because more often than not the assignments are borderline impossible. Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: I don't like bashing on any teachers, but this was the hardest class I have ever taken and I have learned/understood absolutely nothing. Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: Not if it's him or the CS department but the course is poorly laid out. It is the final week and we have 9 assignments in out of 28. Normally you would know if you can pass or not by now.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: If you are new to computer science like me, I would highly suggest you stay away from this professor. His grading is very strict and the homework is nearly impossible to do if you don't have a strong understanding of the material. The most annoying part is the homework is considerably different from the lectures so they are almost no help.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: I left a review for Meade previously, giving him a 2, but now I'm going to give him a 1. It's important to add that, right now, there's 5 days until the final and half of our work hasn't been graded. No clue on the status of my grade. If your only C experience is Intro to C, don't take Meade. His teaching style/workload is for experienced C-ers.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: WORST PROFESSOR IVE EVER HAD. Does not know how to teach. Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A- | Review: He is accessible outside of classes and provides lots of resources to pass. Office hours, example code, SI hours. This class is not easy unless you are confident in your coding and he isn't as clear during lecture. but DO NOT CHEAT. He will tank your grade and does not grade quickly. I have learned a lot from him. He isn't that bad.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: Very chill and personable prof. Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: Ignore all these 1 star reviews, Meade is a very fair and nice professor. The only thing I'm knocking off a point for is very very slow grading. His teaching style didn't hit for me, but that's just me, plenty of people learned a lot from him. Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: I wish I could rate him a 0. If I could go back I would've taken a different professor or waited until next semester. Would rather graduate late than take CS1 with Meade. This class made me hate programming :(

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Hes a young man that knows his stuff. However, he does not know how to teach. He seems to be all over the place and his lectures are not very helpful. This class has complex material that should be taken with a teacher that knows how to teach. I am usually fine with most teachers but this guy cant teach.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: Professor Meade talks faster than Ben Shapiro in debates and crams so much material so quickly and your expected to know everything and put it all together in such a short amount of time. Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: He takes forever to grade assignments. Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: I would not recommend taking Professor Meade if there are other options available. Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: I want to warn you about this class. Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Do not take ever take professor meades class. Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Meade is simply not a good teacher. Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: B | Review: The Professor is very difficult to follow along through lectures. The lectures are very disorganized and not super informative. If you take this class, I highly suggest you have an amazing understanding from Intro to C Programming. I will give the Professor credit for the amount of time he spends in Office Hours. A lot of useful information there.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Meade is an extremely smart guy. Funny, too. He's not the very best at explaining things, though. This class had way too much material and unfortunately, he didn't convey it effectively; he talks way too fast. Our grades mainly consisted of large coding assignments and tests, but that's it. Not a lot of practice assignments to practice material.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: A lot of other reviewers have stated that Meade is not a great Teacher. This is kind of true. Meade talks really fast so it may be hard to keep up and understand what he is saying. Otherwise, he is an average teacher. His homework and exams are challenging but CS1 is a challenging course. If you practice and take advantage of the resources you pass

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Drop/Withdrawal | Review: Ignore the reviews saying he's a good prof. He's not and anyone who thinks he is has never had a good prof. He's a coding genius but has clearly never put in work to improve his teaching skills. If he was 50% worse at coding and 1% better at teaching, he'd be 100% better as a prof. He also makes mistakes during grading. Don't take him.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Meade is a good teacher. If you attend all the lectures, labs, do the homework and utilize other office hours and SI, this class will be a lot easier. I suggest making sure you don't have anything to do right after this lecture, since Meade continues teaching past the lecture time. 8 HW graded tough, 3 Tests a little more lenient, weekly lab quiz

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: B | Review: Very hard class but it's not Professor Meade's fault. He tries his best to help out all of his students and staying after lectures and attending office hours are a must if you want to pass unless you are a coding genius, you will do well in the class if you put in the work. Final grades were curved, a 58% became a C.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A- | Review: Ignore all those reviews about Meade, he has gotten a lot better recently, however, the best part about Meade is that he always stays late after class to help students. Just make sure you study the content well and you will pass his class.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Drop/Withdrawal | Review: DO NOT have him as a professor. His assignments are hard enough as it is and he grades them so unfairly. Majority of the class is failing and he just says to change our study habits. I've had an F all semester despite having good code and doing every single assignment. He doesn't know how to teach this subject, you won't learn with him.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502H | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: B- | Review: Computer Science 1 is basically the bottleneck/cutoff class for UCF CS majors, and it's super difficult, especially for someone like me who had little experience pre-college. Very funny, very helpful outside of class, GO TO THE LECTURES, very clear grading criteria for homework, biggest issue is lack of TAs (just go to non-honors TAs) Very solid

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Worst professor i've had so far. We had an incredibly hard assignment week 1, and same for week 2, which was so hard that my dad who does programming for a living couldn't even figure it out. The online lectures are hard to understand because he teaches us as though we already know it. I will never take him again if i can help it.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3503 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: I took Meade Fall 2020, and I didn't think the class was too bad. There were 7 assignments, each fairly difficult but made easier with provided code and examples. I would recommend being in class as much as possible in order to fully understand each topic. Tests were kind of hard, so I'd suggest studying as much as possible for those

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3503C | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: B | Review: I passed this class by pure luck. Unless you are planning on coding for the rest of your life, this class is not for you with this professor. We had a total of 7 Programming Assignments, 3 lab projects with code and 3 exams. It was nonstop work from day 1, and the professor lacked the proper communication skills to help us. 50 was a cutoff for a C.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COT3100H | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: Professor Meade is a good person to talk to about math, but is not the best professor. He has not taught this course many times, but tries to give his students what he can. The lectures are somewhat unorganized, as he mainly covers the topic through examples. It is best to start his homework early in the week, and go to his office hours.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3503 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A- | Review: I took Professor Meade for CS1 and I can say I was not completely prepared for the foundation exam which was tough. I decided to take him again for CS2 because I like him and it was a pretty good class. Just make sure to study outside of class. Most assignments can be done quickly with proper knowledge and tests are not too bad. Also curved grades.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3503C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: Dr. Meade is legitimately great. He stays for a long time after, very engaging, lectures answering every single question. He has great office hours. My favorite aspect is the assignments are weighted equally to the exams. I do not test well, but I know I can get an assignment finished with enough time and help from Dr. Meade. The course is curved.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: B+ | Review: CS1 with Meade is a bit hard, I'm not going to lie. His penmanship is horrible, he doesn't quite elaborate in simple English when tough topics are taught, and is a bit hard to get a clear answer from. I would recommend taking him again only as I know how he teaches and his grading scale typically changes by 10+ points at the end of the semester.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3503 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: Drop/Withdrawal | Review: Great guy, very likeable, Not a good teacher. Class is poorly organized. Bottom line, find a prof who knows how to *teach* the material. He lectures like hes trying to explain a difficult topic to someone he can tell doesnt care about what he's saying, just flying past confusing areas like 'yeah, theyll just infer that bit im sure'. . .

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COT3100H | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Doesnt provide important details, doesnt connect ideas, bad penmanship, disorganized written lectures, makes mistakes often and goes too quickly. Professor Meade is not confident at all. He seems like a nice guy, but he lacks teaching skills. Im on a zoom lecture right now, in which hes currently struggling to spell the word chocolate.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: C+ | Review: Dr. Meade lectures were interesting and he would often make funny (sometimes awkward) jokes. Personally, his lecturing style went over my head at times - especially the in-class coding. Regardless, he provides lost of extra credit opportunities and SI/TA's + his office hours were really helpful. The assignments are "do able" but stressful.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: By no means is this class a cakewalk, but this will be the case for any other professor. Put time and work in and you'll be okay. That said, Travis Meade is not the most organized lecturer, so you may be left to learn a little on your own. If you visit him after class or during office hours, he can be very helpful and he really does know his stuff.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: A | Review: This class taught me to learn things on my own. While I respect Meade and learned that he is insanely smart, I did not retain anything from lectures. If he somehow sharpens his lecture skills I can definitely see this class being a recommendation. I would only take this class if you want to learn how to learn on your own.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: C | Review: First, please dont be discouraged by the negative reviews you see. Dr. Meade truly cares about his students successjust expect a college level difficulty and realize that this course is required before a computer science fundamentals examination for a good reason. You can pass the class as long as you show your effort where its counted.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: Meade is absolutely one of the worst professors I have ever had. He is a tough grader and sucks at lecturing with his messy hand writing. Labs are a waste of time and didnt help much. The homework assignments aren't easy either and take up a big chunk of your grade. Only take him if you want to suffer, not learn anything, and for your GPA to drop

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: B | Review: Meade is a great professor. It is true that his class is hard, but so is CS1 with every professor at UCF. His exams are fair, although homework assignments are vey tough, but he drops 2 and usually ends up curving the overall grade depending on how the class is doing. He was very helpful during office hourrs and a couple of labs that he showed up

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: C | Review: Literally don't take this professor if your life depends on it. Go to Szum's lectures if you end up with this dude as a professor for anything besides Intro to C. Waste of a semester.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502H | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: Friendly and full of energy, easy to tell he is passionate about his field. He's always willing to help with questions about assignments during his office hours.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3223H | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: C | Review: I came into this class without any programming experience and it was not easy. The class progressed quickly, but its manageable if you put in the time it requires. He will work with you if you go to his office hours. Find a tutor if you can as well. They have free tutoring at SARC.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: I really liked professor Meade. I don't think his lectures are all over the place but he does run out of time very often. he graded us on only a few big assignments but he gave us 2 weeks to do them and he and the TAs were very helpful outside of class. The test averages are low but then again so is the avg for the FE which he takes questions from

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: A | Review: Poor lectures, all over the place. Hard assignments with little practice opportunities. Exams were okay-ish averages for 1-3 exams were 62/74/54. 12% overall curve in place. He helps a lot in his office hours, but sadly you HAVE to go to make decent progress in the course. Avoid if you can, if you get him its not end of the world. You'll be okay.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: B | Review: A very disorganized class. Lectures were all over the place. Some assignments incorporated material that we don't learn in the class. Generous curve however and has the usual TAs in the cave to help whenever.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: If you are a CS Major and need to prepare for the FE or really want to learn well in general, I would not recommend taking Travis Meade. His class is NOT hard. If you study, the tests are VERY fair if not leaning on the easier side. His programming assignments range in difficulty, he drops one, but he is a slow grader. However he is a nice guy.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: Office hours are good for assignments sometimes, exams are very hard and material on exam is unclear most of the time. Biggest complaint is lack of grading. Finals week and I currently do not know what 50% of my grade is. Stinks when the class is curved 10%+ due to most failing exams/homework and its still a struggle to know if you're safe or not.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: Dr. Meade is still figuring things out; I noticed improvement over just the course of the semester. He does not give extra credit, but there were a couple of opportunities for grade replacement. He is very very generous with partial credit on exams. His office hours are exceptional. The assignments are maybe a little too tough, but you learn a lot.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: Let me be clear; I do not dislike Dr. Meade. He is a nice guy and he knows his stuff, he just doesn't know how to teach. If you either already know the material covered in this course or just want to learn on your own and have a class where you can get away with skipping lecture, he is your guy. If neither of those apply to you, DO NOT TAKE HIM.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: He is a phenomenal person, but not the best at actually teaching the material. You can skip his class or go, either way you won't know anymore than before. He is very lenient in that he gives alot of extra credit and curves the grading scale for your benefit, but overall if you are a Comp. Science major and have to take the foundations exam? Avoid.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: Taking Prof. Meade's class inspired me to throw away my entire career in STEM and pursue art!! If it weren't for his advice that I get the hell out of a science-related path before I find myself taking residence under the SR-528/I-4 interchange, I surely wouldn't be as happy as I am today. Make sure you go to class, or you could miss a life change!

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: Drop/Withdrawal | Review: Professor Meade is a terrible professor. A pure example as to why we need something to hold professors accountable. Rambles aimlessly in lecture. Doesn't answer emails. Will leave office hours early if there are too many people asking for help. If you get any help, be prepared to have a -25% on an assignment because it's deemed "cheating." Terrible
Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: Travis a great professor! 11/10 would take multiple times again!

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: He wasn't even the professor I chose for this class but it seems my original professor dropped the course and got replaced with this guy. First day of class told us not to check his RateMyProf. I can't explain the pure rage I feel having to take this class with him. He isn't clear about what he is teaching. Dont take this man for the love of god.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: NO idea of what is he teaching, pull out bunch of codes without explaining them grade home work really hard, does not give partial credits towards work

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: Very disorganized. I have no idea what he is talking about most of the time. He writes these 150 line of code programs and keeps on typing without explaining what he's doing. Expects us to know memory management perfectly. Super confusing. The class is a mess.
Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A- | Review: So inspirational, I would definitely recommend!
Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A | Review: Travis is very passionate and I've learned the most from classes with him.
Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: Great professor! 5/7 would take again.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502H | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: Overall I really liked him. Went a little fast in lectures, and could get off topic because of some kids in my class, but he's a good teacher. Assignments were challenging but I went to office hours for help and he was very helpful. Held office hours on Sundays before homework was due which I thought was pretty cool of him. Generous grading scale.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Literally did not explain what a recurrence relation is and could not explain it in plain English. I had no idea that Discrete would be a subject in this class, so it might as well be a prereq. Travis is unable to explain complicated subjects clearly, and understandable to lay people. If I wasn't already a programmer than I would switch majors.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3503 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Avoid Meade at all costs, this is your warning. You will learn absolutely nothing! The worst professor I've ever had! Waste of time, waste of money.

Professor: Travis Meade | Course: COP3503 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: Dr. Travis Paul Meade is a great professor and lecturer. He gives interesting assignments and the tests are pretty easy. So long as he has good TA support the class is pretty easy and stress-free.
