Assignments are graded a bit harshly, but the tests are fair. Everything he does is fair.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: C | Review: This is a class you really need to take the time to study for and understand the material. If you're not strong in the basics of C programming, I suggest you do not take this class. He'll explain basic concepts but then wildly twist the concept for assignments. Assignments are due nearly every week. Tests are tough. Big curve at end.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A | Review: Very condescending lecturer. Treats students like children/high schoolers. Popular among underclassmen/Valencia transfers, but has a flippant attitude. Does not respect his students. Class was easy to the point of being a joke. Skipped a project and started only showing up for the tests due to his irritating attitude. Easiest A of my undergrad.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT4210 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: I'm sure the professor knows his material, but his explanations of it are very hard to follow. Inquiries for clarification are usually met with a snarky comment. The grading is tough and knowing whether or not you'll pass is near impossible with the standard deviation grading curve. Take COT4210 with another professor and spare yourself some grief

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: C | Review: This class was difficult but he tries his best to explain it and does a really good job. This was my first coding class so it was impossible for me, but if you have a base for coding you'll do fine.  He's been teaching this class for many years so he knows what he's doing and he is incredibly helpful and incredibly nice.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Guha is a nice guy but if you have no programming experience, DO NOT take it with him. He expects the students to become experts after doing 1 program assignment. The program's only get harder and his tests are rediculous. Do yourself a favor and take with another professor. Wish I read these reviews before signing up with him.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Great professor, difficult class for beginners. This class is difficult, but it needs to be because it only gets harder so a good foundation is essential. Expect to read and study a lot and learn even more. Always there for you, and very helpful if you ask. Great experience, but expect to work for it.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: B | Review: If you ask a CS student past hisher first semester at UCF who their favorite teacher is I can put money on the fact that his name comes up. That's the case for a reason. This dude could teach a donkey how to write C. Keyword teach. Your grade will not be handed to you but if he notices that you're working and that you care, he WILL reward you.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP4516 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: B+ | Review: Arup is the definition of how an educator should be! He is always motivated and positive. Always there for you if you need him.  Gives very good feedback and cares about his students. Don't expect his class to be easy but you will learn a lot from him. However, he is very generous with grading.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3930H | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Arup is a great lecturer in a small class like the one I had with him. He's always available for help, and he's going to make sure you learn along the way. This particular class seemed to be a hodgepodge of random (but fun) topics, but he still made it worthwhile.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: Very good teacher. Assignments take a while dont underestimate. Difficulty wasnt too bad. Slight curve at the end. Im super happy that I now have a solid C++ foundation. Would recommend!

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3503C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Arup is the best CS professor I've ever had. I skipped ahead taking this course; it wound up being 1) the most interesting class I've ever taken and 2) the hardest class I'd ever taken up to that point. Know your stuff inside & out for exams, it will not be a regurgitation. That said, you will learn a ton and Arup's lectures are really interesting.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100C | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Stay away unless you are a math wiz and a computer genius... and if you love to do school work instead of eating, breathing, and sleeping. MGF 1106 would be a good class to take in order to understand it at first, but college pre-calc might be the best prereq for it (although it isn't). If you can, take the sophomore-level Discrete Math class.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: Incomplete | Review: Professor Guha definitely knows his stuff and can be a great teacher but the big problem is that his TA's grade everything. Yes they go by his criteria but I swear they're out to make students' lives miserable. When he grades assignments I do fine but when they do I get less than an 80 every time (TA's sign comments when they grade online).

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Great Teacher. Took for Discrete Structures. Probably top 3 teachers in regards to CS. Knows his material and also enjoys teaching.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223COP3502 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: If you try hard you'll be very good at programming fundamentals. Programs aren't fun unless you geek out over math. He's a brilliant programmer, but is also full of himself, has a cult following, and his classes attract trolls. Don't be fooled by the fanboys, he's not the only competent professor here. Don't think you have to take him.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223COT3100 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: I took COP 3223 with him online, and COT 3100 in person. He is the best CS prof at UCF. His HW and tests are challenging but they really teach you the material. I wish I had been able to take him for CS1 as well but he wasn't teaching it that semester.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Helpful professor, but stubborn on the grades. Webcourse quizzes never submitted them even though their was proof of me starting them and I got 4 zeros for them. Also gives practically no pts if it does not compile even if you have 90% of the code. Hard tests but huge curve.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: This class itself is particularly difficult, but he is definitely willing to help you at his office hours. The homework assignments were worth 2% of your grade each. The exams were difficult, but study hard and you should be fine. Also, there is a nice curve, so don't worry too much when you get a 48% on your first test like I did :D

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: B | Review: Homework assignments are super hard (60% average), thank goodness the first two tests are easier in comparison (got C on both). The final exam (test 3) is as hard as the homework assignments though. Definitely do the community service extra credit. Make sure to attend test reviews and go over your notes before the test. Also, very nice grade curve!

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: B | Review: Do not panic if you fail all of the tests. His curve is good enough to help pass the class. I took him for Discrete, and I will be taking him for Computer Science. If he was bad I would not recommend him for myself again.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Very helpful teacher.  Very difficult tests, but a 50 is a passing grade in his classes, so don't drop.  Also a complete babe.  I went to class every session just to see him

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: DONT TAKE. EXTREMELY VAGUE IN LECTURES AND GIVES THE MOST COMPLICATED EXAMPLES FOR ASSIGNMENTS. THE LAB IS A JOKE JUST TA'S DOING MEANINGLESS EXAMPLES THAT DONT HAVE ANY BEARING ON ASSIGNMENTS.  LIKE I SAID DONT TAKE CANT STRESS ENOUGH.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Brilliant and exciting professor. He uses clear examples to explain everything. His tests are super hard and he makes you work for your grade, but he curves the class a lot, and is always there to help you out. Best Computer Science professor I've had yet.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Excellent, dedicated teacher. Willing to work with you for as long as it takes. Incredibly clear and demonstrative lectures. The assignments and exams were great for learning the subject. Exams were too long at first but he adjusted them as the semester went on.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223H | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: Arup knows his crap and literally wrote the book other teachers use. He explains things well and offers help when you need it. Great guy and I would definitely take him again. I got an A relatively easy with little prior programming exp. Just do all the assignments and study what he tells you to. Remember that practice makes perfect!

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: This class wan't easy, and I entered this class with very little programming experience, but I was able to get an A with a lot of hard work. Arup is a very good teacher, some of his lectures are interesting, he sometimes gives examples using people and hands out candies to those who volunteer. His archived notes and code helped me out a lot too.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Programming assignments are not exceptionally hard however the test cases he has the TA's use to grade them have a lot of unexpected input values. Unfortunately for major assignments you are restricted to using C (some TAs even grade with C89). I figured out shortly after the final that a lot of his test questions are taken from the foundation exam

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Over complicated assignments. He said his goal is to make our programs crash, so special cases are always not obvious. He doesn't follow any book. He teaches programming as if it's obvious to most. His class notes are useless. He skips lots of material, yet expects you to know it. He makes the course more complicated than it should be.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: The class average sad, he curves by 20%. He will make all the tests insanely difficult, all the assignments later in the course too become very complicated and not thoroughly explained. His graders will take off 60 points for an inaccurate output, which that cost me a letter grade. I'd rather gouge my eyes out then take another class with this guy.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: I am typically an A student. I work really hard in all of my classes, but even harder in this one, only to have a "D" standing at the current moment. He apparently does curve, but this is one of THE most time consuming classes ever. Most people I know are failing this class currently. TAKE A DIFFERENT PROFESSOR! He's a nice guy, HORRIBLE teacher.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: He says you need no experience prior to his class, however, this is not the case.  He is very very unclear in his directions.  Gives tests where he tells you that the average class grade will be a 60%... Do NOT take this professor. You will regret it. Unless however, you think you are a very very skilled programmer.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: There are two types of IT students, programmers and non programmers. If you aren't a programmer, good luck. I hate everything about programming and this was like learning Greek even after taking C. Arup is fast paced and 80% of students fall behind in his class. If there is another professor more lenient on the programming aspect, take them.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Unless you have had previous programming experience - Try to avoid taking him if you can. There is another professor that teaches this. Also please do not take video streaming it makes it 20 times harder.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT4210 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Try to go part-time that semester and/or cut back on hours at work as the class is going to require far more time than other classes. It'll still probably be the lowest grade you'll ever get, so don't freak out too much.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Professor is rude and insults students. Three years of C programming experience get you a B- in this class. Not recommended.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT4210 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Knows the material. Useless at explaining it. Unwilling to help students without insulting them in the process. Exams are overcomplicated and cover material never mentioned in class.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: CIS3360 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Great professor, really smart and really knows his material. Has a tendency to go off on tangents a bit, but they are at least interesting and relate to the material. I've taken 4 of his classes, and I'd recommend him for any class you can take of his. The classes are hard and you have to work for your grade, but you come out knowing something .

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: CIS3362 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Class was more for math & CS students but as IT I passed with a B. Good amount of coding & professors goes over tons of math proofs that you won't need for exams to pass but it helpful. Clear instructions and kind professor. Huge curve at the end (C- = 59) so you almost can't fail. I highly recommend Arup.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Please keep in mind this rating is for the online coarse. I took the first online Into to C coarse offered by Arup Guha. The coarse was Extremely difficult. If you are a novice or have no experience programing I highly recommend not taking the online coarse. The lectures are only 20 mins max and don't cover nearly enough material. Beware.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Arup is a really great professor if you are considering taking him. He knows how to lecture and help students understand the material. Programming Assignments are very difficult so be prepared to start them very early before the due date. Also his tests are extremely difficult, and can also be very tricky so make sure you read his lecture notes.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Fully awesome teacher! Comp Sci 1. Probably one of the best you'll get at UCF for Comp. Eng.. The tests are HARD and the assignments can take time but aren't too hard. Use the correct compiler to not lose points. He has crisp english and is really good at explaining things. Curves the test grades and offered extra credit. I got a B. Recommended!
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Arup is an AWESOME teacher. He's fun, reliable, smart, and interesting.
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: CDA3103 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: Teacher knows how to teach and explain ideas to students in simple language.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Awesome guy! Lectures are fun, and he bases all the programs around his wedding planning which is humorous. Very reasonable grading, I'm taking him again this summer, definitely recommended.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: If you like programming then his class will be a breeze. His lectures are well planned and he is very approachable. The assignments get difficult very quickly so attendance in class is highly recommended. All notes and examples are posted online which is always a plus. Overall good teacher.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: The lectures are very well organized and he does a good job covering the basics of each topic. However if you haven't had any previous experience with programming (like I have) don't count of his help. He will answer only small, quick questions (in his office). Which is reasonable with a class of 200.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Prof. Guha is probably the best prof. I've had so far at UCF for two main reasons:clarity&accessibility. Lectures&text are clear & interesting.Text overlaps fully with lectures.Half each exam is closed book/tracing;the other half is open book/code writing.Prof. is also very accessible by office hours&e-mail,&has abundant material/past exams on Net.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP2020 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: I've had this teacher for multiple classes, and he is by far the most knowledgeable and helpful professor I have interacted with thus far. He has high expectations for his students, but he also gives them every opportunity to fulfill them and personal assistance when needed.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Had Arup for Intro to C and CS1.  Both courses moved very quickly, but his lectures were very helpful and his assignments really taught you the material. Every CS1 class (whether he's teaching it or not) uses his sample code in the lectures. I would highly recommend you take him for any and all classes you can.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT4210 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Hands down the best instructor you will find at UCF. Lectures are incredibly insightful, coursework is challenging but in the most brilliant sense of the word. You will learn, grow and become a better person taking any of his classes. Look for him whenever possible if you're serious about a Computer Science career.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: CIS3362 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: When you take a few classes in comp sci you will realize Arup is one of the few professors that has excellent teaching skills.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT4210 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: He is brilliant and he presents the material very clearly in a way that is easy to understand. However, he sort of disappeared during the latter part of the semester, which left many students hanging. Still earned a B with little effort.
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP2500 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: Really nice teacher.
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP2500 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Very nice teacher.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT4210 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: I've heard a lot of good things about Arup and I was not at all expecting anything to be true, but he really is one of the best teachers in CS (I think the best). He isn't easy but it doesn't matter because he makes you want to learn and do well. Every class is interesting, though I may be biased because I actually liked discrete one.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Took honors intro to c class. If you don't know anything about C coming in, you will get lost at some point. He teaches new concepts very quickly. Nice funny guy but be prepared to work your ass off for an A. Tests are open book, helps very little

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Fantastic professor. Challenging, you have to go to class and pay attention to master all the content, but quizzes the first few weeks ensure students keep up at the beginning. Six assignments (could do community service rather than the last) that were extremely challenging, but satisfying to complete. Curved the entire class at the end.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: He is hard to follow when introducing new concepts.The speed when introducing new material is way to fast. Since he does not follow the book, its pretty much useless to waste money on it. If you don't have a laptop to take this class, don't take it as its nearly impossible to take notes by hand since this is all coding.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: CIS3362 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Once again Arup is a great CHALLENGING teacher but you definitely learn something which is what i like about him. Assignments were with a partner. Test 2 was the hardest. Curved class-85 was an A! Had extra credit opportunities. He did the comm. service thing again which was good to avoid an extra assign. learn about history of cryptography in here

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: gotta love arup! hes great. i will take any classes he teaches. very clear on what he wants. his exams were half multiple choice and half frq (open book), easy if you did his review. his programs were challenging but really helped learned the material. if you go to class, you will get a good grade. if not, you'll do fine if u do the programs right.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Excellent teacher.  Really cares about his students and wants them to do well.  You will need to study hard for his exams.  As long as you do more programming than the homework you should be fine.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: CIS3362 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: I've had Mr. Arup Guha (do not get mixed up with Dr. Ratan Guha) for 3 classes now (COP 3223 and COP 3502) and he is by far my favorite professor ever. His classes are challenging but they are very fair. For instance the A in this class was an 85 or above. I suggest going to class he discuss quite a bit of information. Highly recommended.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: I'm an EE major and came in with no programming experience. I had no problem with the course. It was a lot of fun, actually. Mr. Guha makes the material interesting, gives it to you in easy-to-manage chunks, and is always clear. I walked out of his lectures ready to apply what I'd just learned, and I even found myself programming for enjoyment.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3503 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: There is a saying about this teacher.  "If you are a CS major, you will appreciate him. If not, you won't."  He expects a lot of you, you need to work hard, but if you are interested in computer science, he will get you to where you need to be.  Great professor.  He cares a lot, and his grading is fair, despite how hard his tests are.
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Okay guy. Grading is weird.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Great professor. I didnt have any programming experience but passed with an A. Provides clear examples in class and plenty of practice programs online. Dont be stupid tho, he challenges you with his tests so dont assume its enough to just go to class. pay attention in class, take time on the hw, and review concepts by messing with some programs.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Arup is the best professor I've been taught by so far. He makes his tests challenging to ensure that students study, but he has an extremely fair curving system. If you're struggling, he will go out of his way to break down something step by step. Definitely looking to take more classes with him. TAKE HIM!

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: This class was SOOOO hard for me.  I tried very hard, and thankfully I passed.  Others had no problem, but I am sure they had more programming experience than I did.  The tests are majorly curved.  He is super nice and helpful. Make sure you visit his office reg.  if you are in one of his classes.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Arup is without a doubt the most interested Professor I've had. He genuinely cares about his students and his interest in the material is infectious. It's not an easy class, but that's not his doing - it's tough material. Curves generously, but not so much so that you can't try. No textbook use. Take Arup, it's really that simple.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Arup is one of the best teachers at UCF. His homework and tests are difficult because he forces you to think in depth about the material he goes over.  I got a B in the class and I wouldn't consider myself an efficient programmer.  This class does require hard work, but you will learn.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Arup is one of the best professors I have come across in my three years of college! He is clear and goes at a great pace. You MUST go to class though. It's not that bad, I'm sure you can endure it. Go to TAs for help on assignments.  Grading is very fair!! He sets his own grade lines depending on the class's performance. Go to cs.ucf.edu/~dmarino. You'll find past grades, comments from students and everything you want to know about arup! I definitely recommend this professor! Take him!

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: He is a great lecturer and gives hard homework, but you are guaranteed to learn in his class. He is extremely clear in explanations.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: If you have experience with C (or other programming) this class will probably be easy. If you haven't had any it can be very tough at times. His tests are tricky, get use to the way he likes to ask output and tracing questions before your first test. Programs are easy in the beginning than get difficult, and take alot of time to write.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Horrible Teacher. He is easy if you know programming before hand, but if you don't you will struggle. Tests are very tricky. He loves to put tricks in his questions. Programs are alright but get hard near the end. Overall, I recommend not taking him if you are new to C

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: He is very helpful. Maybe a little too helpful. He will sometimes come late to class because he was helping students in his class. He does not show up to every class mainly because he is sooooooo busy. I never used his book although his test used open books. I still ended up with an A. Just attend class and he spoon feeds you the material.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Lecturs are very personal, this guy isn't a concrete face, actually has personality. Don't need the book or really need to attend lectures, class was pretty easy. If you've already taken upper division math courses and have an engineering/mathematics mindset, you'll do fine. If you're a retarded business or IT major then you'll suck.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: CIS3362 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: I've had Guha for 4 different classes now and his methods go across all of them. He's not easy, by any means, but he is the first professor at UCF I've felt TRULY cared about his students. He's brilliant, very personable, and is just an all around good guy. I don't care what grade you get, you'll walk out of his class having learned something.
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: This guy is the man!

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: This class was hard for me. But keep in mind I had no previous programming experience and very little interest(took it b/c I had to) compared to other prof he's prob the best teacher, but he's test are Hard. 2test plus final and 6 programming assignments. Dont buy the book, everything for his class is on line.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Awesome Professor!! He is extermely clear and he simplifies EVERYTHING so its very understandable. Is funny at times, but always cool, and never loses focus off teaching. Definetly take him over any other professor for ANY computer science class. Tests are kida hard and some easy, but thats expected.
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Covers material too fast and his tests werent representative of the material he covered.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Cool guy, seems like he has a lot of fun in the class.  Uses a curved grading scale based on the class average.  Programming assignments can be challenging, but you should expect that anyway.  Definitely take Arup for every class possible!

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: So, this is my second class with Arup, and he is a pretty good instructor. Dumbs the lectures down enough for people like me without a strong CS background- explains everything in (sometimes excruciating) detail. Love his style of teaching,and would definitely take his class again.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Good guy, drank with at a tailgating event (gave me free beer), test are hard, and tricky. Dont try to share work, really smart guy he will catch you.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Nice guy. Fun class. gives money to volunteers. exams aren't too bad but need to reason over questions a couple times, he sometimes likes to trick you. overall great professor

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Awesome guy. Very funny and provides detailed, thorough examples of concepts. I have no prior experience with programming and, though I find the class very challenging, it is nothing you can't pass with at least a C, unless you aren't trying.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3503 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: One of the best professors I've ever had.  Courses are challenging, but fair.  Really speaks clearly and at a pace that is easy to follow.
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: best teacher in cs dept. as easy as cs gets, which is not that easy.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Arup Guha is the bomb.  He makes programming so easy and you will learn alot.  If you already have programming background in C then don't take the class because he goes at a kind of slow pace which is good for beginners.  I def would recommend him and for any other courses!

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Pretty nerdy, yet cool guy. He's very knowledable. If you go to class, he does a great job at explaining the material. His tests are fair. He "curves" your grade at the end of the semester. I think a 74ish will get u a B. This is a tough course, but he makes it really clear. I recommend him over any other programming proffesor.
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Good lecture notes, but all the math on the tests got really annoying.
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: He get's really giddy and excited about most anything.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3503 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: This is a hard class and he can be a hard teacher, but he is much better than almost all CS teachers. He cares about his students and will help you whenever you need it. Curves the tests which is great. This class is pretty hard compared to CS1 so you need to put effort into it, but he makes it easier. Got a B which isn't bad considering I'm lazy.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3503 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: very like-able person, but was disappointed with him this semester. grading was bizarre with no correlation to effort and assignment value had no relation to amount of effort to complete. i enjoyed the material but did not appreciate the vagueness & lack of communication in assignments which often lead to poor grades  due to his pass/fail mentality

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3503 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: This class was a lot of work, but you learn the material.  I finished with an A, but I still feel his grading scale is lazy and unfair.  Everything is graded relatively which does not allow everyone to pass with As.  Over half of the class received Cs.  The assignments take a lot of time, but are really helpful.  Do not bother buying the book.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3330 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Arup for the win! Unlike Intro. to C Programming, you will probably want to have the book for Intro. to OO, because the exams contain material which forces you to reference the API, unless you are extremely familiar with it already. The exams are fairly difficult, but as usual, Arup adjusts the grading scale to match the exams' difficulty.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3330 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Possibly the best CS professor in UCF. Writes helpful notes, great speaker, and does grades based on class average and SDs.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: The best math teacher I've ever had. Thought his class was impossible on the first day. Turned out to be much better. Hard class, make sure to review notes online, Tests are hard. Thank God the class is curved. Careful on quizzes. GO TO HIS OFFICE HOURS. If you don't go to class, you WILL fail.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: CIS3362 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Very clear lectures and sounded genuinely interested in the content.  Homework was done in groups of 2 and fairly difficult.  Final exam had some curveball questions, but other two exams were pretty good.  Overall, an enjoyable class and grade was based on mean/median of the class.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Great teacher, hard tests, but the grading scale is adjusted to make up for it.  Uses examples you can actually understand and relate to.  Definitely take him over the other two, you'll actually learn!

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: He is a great teacher.  Both him and his father are two of the best CS teachers at our school.   Take their classes if you have a chance.   Very fair teachers.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Arup is a great teacher! This is a class where you will actually learn! Very Hard Tests But Huge Curve at the end!! This guy is great!

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3330 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: This is my second time with Arup, and once again he did fairly well. Most of the time he was spot on, but ocassionally there were flubs or he would outright say he doesn't know this part of java very well. Regardless he tries his best, and will not test you on something he didn't teach well. Grades are VERY curved, lenient guy. He was usually late.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: EXCELLENT teacher! one of my favorite at ucf. the notes are posted online but going to class makes it so much easier to understand. exams are fairly hard and the programs are challenging but fun. i suggest him 100% if possible.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3530 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Arup was always my favorite CS teacher but I dont recommend him for CS3. Covers material too fast and his tests werent representative of the material he covered. 40% of the first test was based on solving 2 rather tricky programming problems that werent discussed in class at all. Hes still a good teacher and I recommend him for anything below CS3.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: This guy is such a great professor.  The first bunch of programming assignments are easy, the last few are kind of hard, but he grades really easy (something like a 45% in the class is a C) The MC parts of his tests are really tricky, but he gives out a free answer with the last question everytime Free reponse is a piece of cake. Will take him agai

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Great guy, great teacher.  Highly recommended.  I don't think I would have ever liked the subject so much but for his teaching.  Many students find his exams and assignments hard, but he will help you understand the material.  C programming can be challenging and confusing to some of us, and this is the best teacher to learn from.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Very nice guy. Takes a lot to get an A in the course though as his exams are very challenging, but he's a nice guy. Do his work, show up to class (he takes attendance) and do the homework and you should be fine.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3530 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Arup is a fantastic teacher! His tests and homework assignments are challenging, but you learn so much from them. He also has tremendous respect and concern for his students. He isn't out to fail students, so it is easy to pass if you pay attention.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Really good teacher, I cannot stress enough take this professor. He is a really nice guy and acutally teaches the material in a method so you can understand it, also goes over tests and programming assignments and what was suppose to be done on them. Seriously take him you will have a great time.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT4810 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: DO NOT take him for topics!!!! Usually there are two sections of this course.  Guha's course requires WAY more work then either Orooji or Leeson. Try a different section.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: Arup has been both my favorite and my most difficult professor. You never really grasped the concepts until doing the assignments, but there were not a lot of assignments at all (however, I wouldn't ask for more!). Arup was VERY helpful during office hours, and I would definitely recommend him (he speaks english.)

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: hes a cool teacher and likes to do a lot of beer examples lol. the material is covered completely so if u pay attention you should be ok. the only problem i had is the learning environment was horrible plus the lack of assignments made grasping the material difficult. but, hes a good teacher so id recommend him over others.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3530 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Insanely hard, but worth it.  I usually like to skate by with minimal effort whether I learn anything or not.  With Arup its a different story.  He challenges you and is very fair about it.  Always willing to help.  Makes CS interesting.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Very good teacher.  Knows what he's talking about, and can handle almost any question thrown at him.  Those with previous programming experience might be a tad bored, but he covers all the material very thoroughly.  Grades are kind of sparse, but he curves (grading philosophy is on his website).  Definitely a good professor to choose if available.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3530 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: B- | Review: Great professor. It's Comp Sci, so don't expect an easy class, but he makes learning worth it. Often explains topics so they are easy to understand. A lot of programming homework, but in pairs (two people).  As always, do the work and you'll be fine.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Probably the coolest professor I've ever met. He's a good teacher, a really smart guy, and real laid back. He projects well to the class as well.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3530 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: I wish I had Arup for CS1 and CS2, because I learned the most from his lectures and assignments in CS3.  Tests are exceptionally hard, but he grades extremely fair (a 60 may equate to a B).  All around great instructor.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3503 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Arup Guha is one of the best instructors at UCF.  His class is challenging, but he more concerned with your learning of the information than the grades.  He is a fair teacher, who is easy to approach and is willing to help as long as your willing to help yourself as well.  He is one of the only instructors that has not pushed me to a TA for aid.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3503 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: IT majors will have a very hard time with him. Unless of course you are getting a minor or already have a BS degree in mathematics.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: good notes, basically gives you the code for programming assignments beforehand. tests are hard, but liberal curve is always applied, usually A>=80.
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Disrespectful and immature.  His teaching does nothing to prepare you for the tests he gives.  Stay away from this guy.
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3503 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: really helpful.If you don't miss any class and go over the notes at home, you will get an A for sure.
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP4210 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Don't take his classes
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP4210 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: B+ | Review: Biased and judgemental. Avoid if you can.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP35023503 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Guha is the best programming teacher at UCF. Some of the veteran professors could learn a thing or two from him. Guha's test are hard, but he genuinely cares for all of his students. He is always available in his office, and he will help you out grade wise as long as he sees that you are trying. Take him for any CS classes that you can.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3530C | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Somtimes the notes were not completely clear, and more was added to them during the class so make sure to take notes.  The tests were hard, and although a study sheet is allowed, it doesn't really help.  Class is curved at the end.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: horrible professor...if you dont get it his way then you are SOL.  I think he is the most unfair professor that I have ever had at UCF.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Very very helpful, very clear.  Gives tons of examples that he mostly comes up with himself.  I had no previous programming knowledge and got an A-.  Grades according to class performance, not material.
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3503H | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: This was my second class with him, a very easy professor to understand, but kind of hard class.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Very good professor.  You will be bored stiff if you already know the material, but he really knows how to break it down for everyone else.  He will always stop what he is doing during office hours for you and he and his TA's always answer emails.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: The material isn't easy, but he does a good job in going over it. I had no computer programming experience beforehand and got a B. The beginning of the class is very easy and the last two or three weeks are hard. Be sure to do well on the tests so you dont have to worry about the last 2 assignments.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Dont listen to these uneducated fools who say he is hard.  He is only hard if you cannot program at all and have no place being in CS to begin with.  He is one of the best CS teachers at UCF and I would highly recommend him to anyone.  Tests are hard but fair, as well as the programming assignments.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3503H | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: This was my second class with Arup, and he didn't dissappoint what I had come to know and love about his teaching style.  He's expert at explaining tough concepts, and you've gotta love a teacher that WANTS you to call them by their first name.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: He makes great lecture notes that he posts online, stresses the important concepts in class, and gives ample examples that are often amusing. Tests are tricky, but as long as you do the sample ones he provides, you know what is coming. He is very helpful if you ask. Great instructor!

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Even though my class was only once a week for three hours he managed to provide an ample amount of samples for his assignments. His grading policy is one that I'd recommend to all the other teachers.He did put a few problems on one of his tests before he taught the material but overall a great teach
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Not_Sure_Yet | Review: A very bad professor.  His tests were a nightmare and well beyond what was taught in class.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3503 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: I also had him for Intro to C and i work for him.  Hes a really nice guy and really helpfull.  And you can understand him, and as most CS majors know that its hard to find a professor that you can understand.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: This is my first programming class, so having no previous knowlege, I was a bit worried.  Guha made everything very easy to understand, and made even the most complex topics seem easy.  Tests may seem tricky, but just look over sample tests he gives you before the real thing. Grading policy nice too

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: I felt he was a horrible teacher and that his tests were unfair.  I would never take him again and think everyone should avoid him.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: Amazing teacher, he has a way of explaining complex topics in a manner that allows you to understand it the 1st time you hear it and most importantly allows you to remember it. I didn't buy the book, never studied, and got an A both class I had him for.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: He is very easy to understand and talk to.  Assignments are usually short.  Need to come to class as much as possible, even if you know C!!!!  Gives make-up exams/quizzes without much question.  If you found the class hard, you prolly skipped alot.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: He gives extremely difficult programs and tests.  You can expect all kinds of curves and tricks with him.  If you dont understand it exactly as he intends it, you get nowhere.  I know several people who have changed majors because of him.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Very good teacher, covers the material very well and speaks clearly. He knows what he is talking about.  His tests are very difficult, a lot of unexpected twists on the test.  Programs are a little bit hard but he gives you enough time to do them.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: His lectures are very clear and structured.  He's a really good teacher but his tests are incredibly tough!  He uses his own grading scale (determined at the end of the semester).

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Speaks clearly, uses a lot of examples, programs are a tad over-board. Don't this course along with another programming course.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Awesome CS1 Teacher, very smart, and actually explains the material very well. Class is tough, but if you study you should be good.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Most helpful/explanatory programming teacher I've ever had. Grades on tests/programs may be bad, but he curves accordingly and generously. Really helps students understand material. I will take more courses with him if at all possible. I recommend!

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Extremely nice and helpful, he may be the best in the CS department...at the same time, his tests are very difficult and are based almost completley on reading trick questions. Huge curve tho.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT4210 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: You may think you have an understanding of the material, but he throws massive curves on the tests which leave you asking where he got this.  If you are willing, learn with another professor and dont waste your time.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Excellent teacher, but can be hard on tests.  Arup teaches a lot more about C and tries to make sure that the whole class understands what he teaches that day.  However, if you're not willing to learn, you shouldn't bother.
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: if you dont get it his way, you wont get any credit on the tests.  Think like him or else!
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: unreasonably hard
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: No Comments

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: VARIOUSCS | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Arup is not only one of the best CS teachers I have had, he is also one of the coolest. He pushes students to work harder but also explains concepts fully using concepts that we already understand. Great guy, I take as many classes from him as I can! :)

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: class is the hard no doubt but he is good at what he does but if its ur first programming class you may want to save your GPA now but if you do take it study study study and take advantage of all of the TA's office hours they were the only reason i passed

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: Class is curved a lot.  If you practice, you will do well.  If you know C, you don't have to go to class :)  Just keep up with the homework, and go to the reviews, and you'll get an A

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: He was great at explaining the C language. If you attend class you WILL understand how to program in C, he goes over in depth EVERY aspect of the code and techniques he expects you to know. Tests are definetly tricky but he gives you a massive curve.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: He is very good at keeping such a boring class fun. He would make a good babysitter too, so many whiney kids in the class needing their noses wiped. By the way if you are a know it all about programming type, keep your mouth shut and just nod already!
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Good intro to comp sci class

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Arup is a hard tester. He is an excellent teacher. He does a superb job of explaining things and showing how things work. He makes students into real CS majors who actually know stuff instead of just get by. he spends money on food for us too! woohoo

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: VARIOUSCS | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Best teacher in the department, bar none.  Too bad you can't take all of your CS from him...you'd actually learn something

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT5405 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: great teacher ... should take more grad classes .. wish i had some one like him in my undergrad classes..makes the tough class so fun and easy to understand....
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: CAP5937 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: ST: Cryptography.  Hard class but great teacher.  I never fell asleep in his class... that's very good!

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: One of the top 3 CS teachers I had during my undergrad at UCF. It's hard to appriciate how good of a teacher Arup is; until you've experienced a poor teacher teaching the same subject. Really cares about teaching, and it shows. He speaks perfect english.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: This is such a hard class! I had never programmed before and felt really stupid the first few weeks. He is an extrememly nice teacher and helps you out a lot though. You definately have to work hard.
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: tests make your brain hurt but are curved heavily.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: His class is very difficult, but he tries to make it easier to understand.  His class notes are posted on the web and grades on a curve.  Stick with the class, but you can't be lazy.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: EXTREMELY HARD!! If you haven't taken a programming class before than you are in trouble. I had to WITHDRAWAL from it after the first program and test because my grades were sooo low... If you turn anything in wrong like .doc instead of .c you get a -1!!

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3530 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Had him for COP3503, COP3530, COT3100, and now COT4810.  Extremely approachable, friendly, and honestly cares about whether or not his students learn.  Brings confusing concepts down to a level that is understandable (ex. 12 permutations of the word beer)

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Awesome teacher.  Puts class notes online.  Very difficult class, but he is extremely good at presenting the material.  Perfect english.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3530 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Had for cs2, cs3, and discrete.  Very good teacher.  One of the best by far.  Cool Cool guy.  Hard classes and hard tests, but he grades on class average.
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: No Comments

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3503 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Very good teacher.  Always at least 5 minutes late for class, but that might just have been my class.  Tests are hard, but I haven't taken a CS course with easy tests yet.  He's good at explaning things and very helpful during office hours
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3530 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Difficult teacher but you'll leave the class knowing a lot.  The curve also helps!

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100H | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: One of the few CS professors who isn't terrible.  Nice guy, hard class, nice curve.  Take this guy as much as you can.  He's the only one with personality.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3503 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Hard tests, way too smart for his own good. Very nice. Amazing curve. Very cool guy, invites all students of drinking age out for a beer after the final.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: great teacher w/ great personality and teaching style.  however, concepts are near impossible to grasp and so are the tests.  the best part is that he grades on a curve.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100H | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Very hard teacher. the tests are awful. but every one in the class stuggles so it's not as bad. Speaks perfect english so you can't make that an excuse
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: LOVE HIM!!! A little tough, but who doesn't like it that way ;)

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: DISCSTRUC | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Ok, it's true that his tests are hard. But his lectures are clear and understanding so, he kind of has a right to make them hard. Great sense of humor, and loves Latitudes :)
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: No Comments

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3503 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: I agree - the tests are horrible, but the curve makes up for it.  So far, he's my favorite teacher here at UCF.  Hopefully UCF will tenure him before they lose him.

Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Great professor...a little tough, but you learn a lot.  At the end of the class, the grading scale is adjusted to be fair (since nobody can pass this guy's tests)
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: best teacher in existence... but only if you actually want to learn...
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Makes some tests impossible, but grades on an awesome curve!
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: too smart for his own good.
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT3100 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: 51% C? Sure why not.
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COT4210 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: All around cool guy. Challenging exams, but grades on a curve! Great teacher.
Professor: Arup Guha | Course: COP3503 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: awesome teach
Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4603 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: Simply the best professor and course combination at UCF.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4714 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: Project based class, no groups. He is an effort-based grader: as long as you show that you even tried, he will give you credit (not an A). His lectures are online, recorded and in person. Highly recommend going in person. One of the only classes where I actually learned. Highly recommend his class. Easy to reach and very approachable.
Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4714 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: That's why he's the Goat! The Goat!

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4714 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: One of the best professor's at UCF. We were only graded on 4 projects, dealing with GUI interfaces (Java) and having to connect them to MySQL databases (also need some basic html knowledge). Involved a decent amount of coding, but the Professor gave psuedo-code to help us out, and responds to any questions quickly. Zoom option available. 10/10

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4714 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: Absolute gem of a professor. There's 4 projects that somewhat build off of the previous one in terms of knowledge. They weren't extremely difficult but they do take a fair amount of time. I hadn't looked at Java for over a year so I didn't remember much but his first project is very forgiving in that sense. Very nice grading. The rubric is clear.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4603 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A | Review: Very easy class. Around 5-6 projects which he gives you the exact directions to and even provides Q&A videos going over them live. Understanding when people had issues and was willing to extend deadlines if needed.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CIS3921 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: Llewellyn maintains a relaxed and flexible teaching style. Course grades are primarily based on attendance during scheduled Zoom meetings, with a requirement to attend 75% of the 12 presentations. In cases where students were unable to attend live sessions, the option to watch recorded Zoom meetings and still receive credit was provided.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4603 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: The class went smoothly, consisting of 6 Projects (each step broken down fairly well) one of those projects being a very long and tedious Excel spreadsheet, but Llewellyn and his great set of TAs helped students out immensely and set a great example of how all college professors should teach and support their students. Would definitely take again!

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4714 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A- | Review: As a 33 year old who was finally able to go back school and finish get my BS in IT I can highly recommend this man as a great professor. Due to my busy schedule I was never able to attend the class but watched his recorded lectures and relied heavily on his lengthy pdfs to complete the projects assigned to us. This course has renewed my love of IT.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4714 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: Mark Lewellyn teaches MANY UCF classes, and he's incredible. For CNT4714, our entire grade came from four projects with no exams. You had weeks to make them, they were fun, and usually 10-25 hours per project. Besides that, there was nothing else to do, so it was a light class, a ton of fun, and the professor was amazing. Take him if you can.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4714 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: I took this class over Summer. There were 3 projects. They revolved around SQL workbench and Java. Takes some time but he is good about helping. Grading is also lenient IMO. Good Luck!

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4603 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: Really entertaining and easy class, you only have 6 projects where you just follow instructions. No homework, no tests. Amazing class.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CIS3921 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: You just need to be on the zoom with your actual name clearly displayed. You have to make a minimum number of zoom meetings to pass. Its just satisfactory or not satisfactory so as long as you are on the zoom the required amount you will pass.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4714 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: Llewellyn is one of the best professors out there. Graded by 4 projects that increase in difficulty/time-taken. Projects were very well rounded, but do take some time. However, he posts Q&A sessions that give many hints and pseudo-code to get you through. It may be challenging if you're not comfortable in Java, but it is doable.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4603 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A | Review: Llewellyn is a great professor. Graded by 6 projects. The only one that takes some time is the excel project, but all projects are fairly straightforward. He hosts and posts Q&As to help you with projects, and provides very detailed step-by-step instructions. Learned a lot about system administration and VMs in a practical application.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4714 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: Class has 4 increasingly complex Java projects and no exams. Depending on your coding background, this class might be hard: you HAVE to put in the work, or else you won't pass. Fortunately Llewellyn is a boss and gives you all the help you need in his lectures and special "Q&A sessions" he hosts on non-class days. I'd highly recommend taking him!

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4603 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A | Review: Prof Llewellyn is easily one of the best professors I've had in my whole college experience and I cannot recommend him enough. He provides great resources for information and is very clear & cut with assignments. Super easy to understand and helpful.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4603 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A | Review: No tests/quizzes, Dr. Mark Llewellyn is an excellent professor, accessible and helpful. 6 Projects: 1 summary, 1 Spreadsheet time-consuming (No hard numbers, ONLY expressions allowed), 4 projects to configure virtual machines (Virtual Box provided). No book, he supplies P.Point to follow. His Q&A meetings will guide you through assignments.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4714 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A- | Review: Graded only on the 4 projects which are pretty difficult, but an extremely easy grader which is nice due to the difficutly of the projects. Has lots of office hours to discuss the projects. Attendance is not mandatory. Very caring and understanding-- willing to work with you if you miss a deadline.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4603 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: A very caring guy that was almost always readily available through Zoom office hours and email. We only had four projects for the whole semester each of which were not very difficult. It took me on average an hour and that was with me being distracted while watching Youtube or completing another task, overall easy class but graded by a few things.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4714 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A | Review: This class is super easy with this Professor teaching it. Read the instructions, complete the projects, and submit the screenshots. Take this class if you're a CS major looking for what to use as your IT elective.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CIS3921 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A | Review: Easy class, as all of these reviews have mentioned - show up to most of your class sessions and you pass. The professor himself is very easygoing and communicative.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4603 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: Assignments were interesting and helpful to a future in IT. Great guy, very caring. Tutorials and walkthroughs were fantastic. Never had an issue with his assignments. One of the projects given is very time consuming so set aside proper time for it. Some excel knowledge is required to feel comfortable in class.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4714 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: You're not good with Java? Show up to class and Q&As. He puts in a lot of work to help you to even straight up offering portions of code to fill in. Four projects + extra credit. In short, create Java GUI programs + utilizing mySQL & Tomcat. Super forgiving to submitting late despite syllabus (instead of -30 penalty, only got -10). Easier than CS1.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4603 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A | Review: Project only class with tutorial instructions. One 2-pg summary, one excel spreadsheet (the most time consuming), and three Oracle VM projects that all took less than an hour. Has lots of office hours if you do need it. We were supposed to have 6 projects, but there were unfortunate complications with the course. Easy A, not time consuming at all.
Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4603 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A | Review: solid class. Very easy but practical material and is accessible.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4603 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A | Review: I wrote a review in December 2022 for Professor Mark Llewellyn when I took Enterprise Computing, detailing how he exerts effort in helping students. Just took System Administration, same thing. 5 projects, 3 of which were configuring virtual machines using Virtual Box. No exams or quizzes. Go to his Q&A if you need 1 on 1 help. Best IT professor.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CIS3921 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A | Review: We just had to attend virtual presentations on Zoom from different IT companies. We had to attend a minimum of 10 presentations to pass. The only problem for me this time around was that a bunch of the presentations got canceled, so I wasn't sure if he would still give us those points. The only con is he hasn't updated the grades for attendance.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4603 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A | Review: Not bad of a professor! Took him as a requirement from FIU and by far a great teacher. No exams, lectures or HW only projects which comprise of using virtualbox virtual machines. First two projects had to do with essay and an excel spreadsheet, 3rd is where it gets interesting! Overall highly recommended.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4714 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: Lectures were pretty good, and the professor was really great! This class taught me so much about multi-tiered applications. Great stuff for your github, resume and java experience. Projects build on one another, and they're really not that hard. I did most of them in one sitting, but with a lot of coffee (you have 2/3 weeks to do them).

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CIS3921 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A | Review: Once a week, one hour presentations by different IT related companies. Attend 10 to pass the course. No homework, no quizzes, no tests. Just show up and sit through the zoom call from the comfort of your home. You missed a presentation? Email him, he'll tell you to just watch the zoom recording, then give you credit. Very easy to do on the side.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4714 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: I'm not good at programming. I suck at Java. But I got a 90 on Project 1 and a 100 on Project 2 & 3. Still waiting for Project 4 to be graded. Professor Mark Llewellyn exerts a lot of effort to help you with the projects, just show up to the Zoom office hours and Q&A for 1 on 1 help. Best IT professor at UCF. He made Enterprise Computing doable.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4714 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: Amazing teacher. Very kind and caring. He is like a father figure to his students. Enterprise Computing is a challenging class, but he taught me so well that I feel accomplished when completing his projects. He is also very willing to help students outside of class. I took him almost 2 years ago and now graduated but I miss him.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4603 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: Took this course as a CS major for an easy tech elective. It is easy... but tedious. Arduous. Checked in every 2 weeks to waste my day writing a business document, working on an excel sheet, or following a powerpoint's point-and-click instructions dealing with crappy software that crashes and resets your progress. Free A, just a pain in the behind

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4714 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: This class was very easy, but did give a good introduction into utilizing MySQL and Tomcat. Our summer term only had 3 projects, and they weren't too bad if you know where to find information online. The professor always extended deadlines and the only time you need to go to class is if you want to ask questions about the project. No exams.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4714 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: I have enjoyed his classes, but this one was the worst thing I have ever taken. No proper lectures, just straight programming in Java. He does QAs which are some what helpful to get an idea on what to do but doesn't really feel enough. Class is literally you teaching yourself how to code everything with little to no assistance.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CIS3921 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A | Review: Easiest class I've taken yet. It's just listening to various professionals in the IT industry. Attendance is not graded as long as the majority of students show up. No assignments or exams. Only thing to keep in mind was lectures would start a few minutes earlier than the course's scheduled time.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CIS3921 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A | Review: Basic careers in IT class which informs you about future internships and what to consider for a career in the IT major. Just show up to lecture, pay attention and that's your grade right there. Had to attend at least 9 lectures to pass the class. Easiest class to knock out the way.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4714 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: Literally such a bad class. It was asynchronous. He never lectured, he would just upload projects and do Q and As about the project. He would just upload like an 80 slide powerpoint to try to figure out. Literally learned absolutely nothing useful in this class. I wish he would put in more effort to actually make this an educating class.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4603 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A | Review: Fairly easy class, one of the few classes I enjoyed due to the content of the homework actually having you to make servers and create domains in the servers. The lectures are there but feel very useless due to the assignments not fully pertaining to them and having a clear criteria on what is required that the lectures become useless.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4603 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: Awesome class if you want to learn about Windows server. Projects are straight forward and easy to do. Pretty easy if you actually care about this kind of stuff. If you do, take him and you won't regret it. No tests or quizzes, just actual hands on work that will translate to the real world.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4603 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: Easy A. The last project's instructions seem to be incorrect, you'll run into errors following his configurations. The project also has python scripting that felt unnecessary. You'll learn the bare basics of Windows Server, and the projects set the bar low. However, the slides seem more informative and I'll definitely be keeping them for reference.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4714 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: Class is based on 4-5 java projects. We were supposed to have 5 projects, but because of the hurricane, we had 4. TA's were not much help, so go to his office hours instead. Make sure to submit your SCREENSHOTS. TA's will give you a zero and then you have to email them and then resubmit them and wait for them to correct your grade; it's stressful.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4714 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: Excellent professor! The entire grade is based on 4-5 projects give throughout the semester. Make sure you have a decent grasp on Java before taking this class. The lectures aren't mandatory and almost all the info you need will be on the powerpoints he posts. Whenever students expressed concern on due dates, he always extended it.
Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4603 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: All homework.. no tests. lectures are clear

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CIS4004 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: Class was only 6 projects. Easy if you follow along. Currently taking my third class with the professor. A little google research or sifting through lectures goes a long way.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4714 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Great prof. Class is graded entirely by 4-5 projects, no exams! As long as the project works you get an easy 100. The projects are interesting and challenging but not too difficult and you can end up with a 100% in this class without much work. Projects are mostly java. I would recommend knowing how to make java user interfaces before taking this

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CIS3921 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: Easy going professor, show up to class 9 times and you pass. He just sends around a sign-in sheet that you sign and then you can leave... at least that's what i did. Passed with a 100% A.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4603 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: Awesome professor! His instructions are very clear and his lectures are great. 10/10 would take him again.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4714 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: His powerpoints are the reason why I was able to do all the homeworks and learn everything that I did. His programs are very useful and interesting. Would take him over anyone.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4714 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: Really great teacher, will sign again. You cant go wrong with this class. Follow all the instructions and look over the PowerPoint. Email TA/Professor if you are experiencing errors in VM. Sign up

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4714 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: This is a heavily project based class. There are no exams or quizzes, just projects that test your knowledge on the topics discussed. Took the professor for three classes and enjoyed all of them.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4603 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: No tests, all easy assignments. He's very insightful and his lectures are very informative if you ask a lot of questions. He gives great, practical, IT advice often. I wish he pushed his students a little harder, but if you have the initiative to push yourself, he's a great professor for helping you do that.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4714 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: This is my third class with this professor and he gives the best notes. I legit only went to two classes and I have an A in the class. His notes make it easier to do the projects. There are 5 individual projects in the semester that are fairly challenging but you have plenty of time to figure them out. No exams or other assignments. He is the best!

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4714 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Took him this summer, there aren't test just projects, and if you attend class or read the slides you should be able to figure the assignments out.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4714 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: Dr. Llewellyn is an awesome professor, but his lectures are boring.
