I have taken him for three classes and he is very helpful. He doesn't believe in giving tests and only gives a few programs, which you could solve just by going through his examples. Take him if you get the chance.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4603 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: Best professor at UCF. Shows up every day ready to teach the material. A lot of people skip out on his class but it's they who will work for me when all is said and done. Show up, his material is well worth the time YOU pay for.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4603 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A | Review: He doesn't take attendance and there are no exams. All of your grades come from projects. The first one is a somewhat difficult Excel cost sheet but after that you either do very simple tasks on a virtual machine (like setting up a virtual network) and take screenshots or write basic scripts. He's pretty generous with bumping back due dates too.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4714 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: No exams or attendance; your grade is based on  5 programming assignments throughout the semester. His programs are simple in concept but can be tricky if you don't know specific things in Java (like GUIs or mySQL). Luckily he gives A LOT of sample code, you can usually reverse engineer the solution from that. He tends to push back due dates too.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4603 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: I took him for Systems Administration over the summer. No tests and attendance is not required. The first assignment was the most difficult and worth the most of the entire semester, after that the class is pretty much an easy A as long as you follow his instructions. Would take again.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4603 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: He's the best! I took System Administration & Maintenance as a transient student at UCF. (FIU is my home institution) He saved my semester! Best professor ever! Shame he is not teaching at FIU because we need more professors like him. UCF is lucky to have him.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4603 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: Half our class ditched out during the semester because attendance was not mandatory. But I showed up to every class. He makes them interesting by applying real world scenarios to the material. FINALLY a class useful for my future.  Project based class, so no tests which was nice. The projects took some time if you didn't know what to do.  FUN CLAS

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4714 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Course grade based on five 100 point Java assignments. Llewelyn is a great teacher I'm sure, though I barely attended class. All lectures posted online, assignments every 2-3 weeks, depending on difficulty. Most of them were extended from students waiting until last minute and complaining. Attendance not necessary unless you need help.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CIS4004 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Professor Llewellyn is the best.  Some of the later topics can be tricky, but his teaching style and examples really clear things up.  One of the best IT teachers at UCF.  Very knowledgeable.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4603 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: A practical class where the assignments actually teach important computer enterprising skills.  No tests because Prof Llewellyn believes you learn more from doing than regurgitating facts, and he's right.  His notes are stellar.  One of the best professors at UCF.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CIS4004 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: Although I barely went to class due to my work schedule, his notes were so clear and concise that I could teach myself everything. Definitely gained a greater understanding of Web based languages (HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript) and never got below a 96 on hw. NO EXAM, ONLY HW which worked perfectly for my schedule. Extremely helpful during office hours!!

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: COP4710 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: Great professor, I had him for Enterprise Computing and again for Databases. Mark Llewellyn is very informative, extremely helpful in office hours, pretty lax in grading and balances his projects and assignments between theory and real world application. Excellent professor.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4714 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Probably one of the best professors at UCF. Enterprise Computing isn't an easy class, but luckily Llewellyn is a very knowledgeable and helpful professor. Get help as early as you can with the assignments. If you wait to get help, there's a good chance of not finishing because you'll get stuck. It'll be good to know some java and GUI.
Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4603 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Good professor. Explains everything clearly. No tests, grades are based on the assignments.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4603 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: Great Professor with excellent powerpoints and clear assignments. First assignment was a cost sheet in Excel that stunk but the reset was follow the pictures or basic coding. Very easy, I never went to class and learned with his ppt's. Nice guy, quick to help, and easy A/B with NO EXAMS!

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: COP4900 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: I've taken him for three classes at UCF.  Probably the easiest professor out there.  No tests, no book, and the way his powerpoints are, you basically can teach yourself.  Has no issues with emails or meeting you if you're having an issue.  Very understanding and a very good professor.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CGS2545 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Though he can be a bit monotone and drone on a little, he is a great professor to have. His lectures are very clear and relevant; he never tests on material not covered in his lecture/on his powerpoints. Always willing to answer questions and willing to admit if he's made a mistake. I would recommend him.
Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CGS2545 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: Very clear and knowledgeable, assignments are well defined and not hard. Lectures are boring. NO funky accent!

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: COP4600 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: Even though this is a senior level class, it is still only an intro class. Material is very basic and easy to comprehend. Only 2 homework assignments and a really easy program. Dr. Llewellyn is an excellent teacher that I would like to take again. Easy A. Don't bother going to class though, just spend a little extra time studying the slides.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: COP4600 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: This might seem like a good review because of the scores, and yes he is a good teacher, but I totally feel like we aren't even learning anything. We had our first test and I thought how the hell is any of this stuff important? It's almost all memorizing. I think I'm getting more use out of my Discrete 2 class with Arup, and it's even more fun!!

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CGS2545 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Very good professor. Very helpful if you dont understand. No textbook. tests were difficult but if you study they are reasonable. assignments were really easy also. I recommend him

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: COP3330 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: I don't think he teaches very well at all. The class seemed very easy and understandable, but come test time it was a totally different story. The same questions would be asked changing the variables only slightly, so getting one question wrong usually meant you got like 3 others wrong too. Should have relied less on slides.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: COP3330 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: I like the way he teaches the class, he takes the time to answer questions and he seems to really know his stuff. I will try to take his classes from now on. A++ Prof

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CGS2545 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Llewellyn is once again a good teacher with the use of powerpoints. I found his tests to be a little harder in this class than previous classes with him. Labs are optional to attend (you still need to do them) Fair tests. Still awaiting on final grades due to an illness he has. Take him! keeps stuff the same from semester to semester

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CNT4714 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: One of the nicest and most understanding professors at UCF.  Like stated in other comments, he can sometimes sound a little monotone, but other than that, he is pretty clear in the concepts and is willing to spend all the time needed helping out students.  Tests come straight from the notes/lectures.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CGS3175 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: Good Teacher, detailed powerpoints. Assignments big part of grade which was good. Best Powerpoints. Was basically web design with xhtml and css. Very easy. only missed about 13 points in whole class out of prolly 1000. Take him he is very helpful

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: COP4710 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Super nice guy, but can be a little monotone at times.  Tests are very similar to homework problems.  Since he usually has quite a few "bonus" slides in his presentations though, it is sometimes a little confusing since you don't know which material you should know.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CGS3175 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: I enjoyed the class.  The assignments were extremely helpful in boosting my grade as well as learning the material.  The material was explained a lot more fully then some of my other computer classes.  Having such detailed power-points online is helpful.  I had to study a little harder than normal for the tests, but they were still decent.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: COP4610 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Dr. Llewellyn is an OK teacher.  His lectures are kind of boring as he reads verbatim from PPT.  However, he is VERY helpful outside of class and will spend time helping you if you go to him.  Tests were good, but not impossible.  Assignments were practical and would be useful for a career outside of college.  Overall he is a B+ teacher.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: COP4610 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Good teacher, knows his stuff.  Dr. L is always willing to help if you come to him.  Classes are a bit boring because his lecture style is reading PPT notes.  Good teacher and would take him again anytime.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: COP4710 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: Great professor.  Took him for this and COP 4610 and wish he taught more.  He's really nice guy and a good teacher. The lectures are a little boring, but since everything he teaches comes off of the notes, which he posts online, attendance isn't necessary (if you can learn well on your own). Fair tests, decent assignments, no quizzes.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CGS3175 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Detailed and great ppts. Easy and fun HW assignments (they help your grade A LOT and he's really lenient on the code of them.)By far the most helpful professor out of class ever. Emailed me back within hours, willing to explain everything, and just really cared about my learning. Final was a breeze but look out for his first tests. Take mark!

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: COP4610 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Teaches off very good PPTs, willing to push due dates by a couple days, tests are largely multiple choice and can be a bit hard.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: COP4710 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Boring class, but he does his best to get all the information across. Great teacher. His homework was really easy, tests were somewhat hard. One of the top CS teachers. Can't decide Arup or Mark? If you want to learn and get the grade you deserve...Mark is the man.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: COP3330 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Had him for OOP in Java. Learn Java or C++ before taking this course. COP3330 is not for learning Java. Didn't really care for the test format. 10 True/False (2pts per) 7 Fill in the blank (2pts per). The rest was analyzing and writing code. Bold text in his notes are bold for a reason - memorize it for the T/F and fill in the blank portions.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: COP4710 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: His notes were extremely detailed to the point where buying the book is useless. He is a great professor and will basically tell you exactly whats on the tests (no surprises, fair exams). The course assignment is to build whatever database you want to---so you actually have the option of making something useful. Fun course, good professor.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CGS2545 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Textbook not needed. He teaches 100% off of powerpoints, but he's the best powerpoint user i've seen in my years at UCF. Tests are 100% from PPTs, so print them out and study them as you go through them in class, will make the tests so much easier. Amazing professor though, so clear and helpful. Lab is a bunch crap assignments, but easy too.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CDA | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: One of the best professors I have ever had while @ UCF! Great teacher who alwqays enjoyed chating with his students durring office hrs. Just use common sense...

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CGS3769 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Outstanding professor. Clearly, the best in the entire CS/IT department. He knows how to present his material, and the material that he does present is always very concise and to the point. He can get you to understand. In addition, he's extremely helpful in and outside of class. He's a bit tough, but you learn a LOT. I can't recommend him enough!

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: I'll be completing my CS degree this semester, and still have very few professors who I would recommend to another student.  That being said,Dr. Llewelyn is, hands down, the best professor in the CS department!  I've had him for all three CS courses and learned a lot from him.  I think that he should teach a class on teaching to the rest of the dep

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: COP3530 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Great Clean notes, Very realistic expectations, fair tests and very helpful outside of class.  One of the best professors in the CS department!!!
Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: COP3530 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: B+ | Review: Great prof his notes are awsome
Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: COP3530 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: notes are probably the best I've ever seen a professor have. very clear descriptions and examples. take him!
Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CSICSIIOOP | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Excellent prof. Clear, clean notes and lectures

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: COP4610L | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Easy class if you keep up with the programming assignments.  His tests are very hard but he will curve them to make up for it.  If you get the chance to take any of his classes do so.  You won't regret it.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: COP4610L | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: Best Teacher in the CS Department! Tests are a bit tough, but he is always willing to help. You will learn more in any of his classes than from every other teacher at UCF Combined!!! Wait, Has he ever had a bad rating???
Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: ALL | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: The best professor at UCF. period.  His test are challenging but you will not regret taking his class.
Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: COP3503 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: MARK IS THE MAN! IF HE IS TEACHING A CLASS YOU HAVEN'T TAKEN YET, TAKE HIM ITS JUST THAT SIMPLE

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: COP4610L | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: I learned more in one semester, in one class, than I had in my previous four years in the Computer Science program. He teaches, in this course, things you really will use, teaches you a lot, makes sure you understand, and is more than willing to help you as much as you need. Excellent course.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Very good teacher, but he needs to be more clear on what your grade is. It is not acceptable to have no clue until the end of the semester.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: COP3330 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Like everyone else said, THE best CS teacher at UCF. His lectures can be boring since he reads over his notes, but they can be found on his website. I didnt get the book and passed the class only using his notes. Great Teacher.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: AWESOME TEACHER!!!! He explains everything very well and has a lot of examples with answers for every topic. Plus, all his notes are on the internet. I would recommend taking any class he was teaching.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: COP3503 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: All his notes are on the powerpoint slides and every test was fairly easy except for the final.  All-around the best CS teacher at UCF. Take him.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: COP3503 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: He was very clear and gave very detailed notes.  Actually, I didn't even need the book.  He can become a bit monotone but he's still easy to follow.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: COP3330 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: One of the best CS professors at UCF. I highly recommend taking his classes. He puts the notes on PowerPoint and pretty much just goes over them in class. He gives great programming assignment descriptions but tests are moderade on difficulty.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: COP3503 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: There is a god!! Or atleast some who is willing to help.  I will take any class he teaches.  He is down to earth and hands DOWN the best at UCF!!!

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: COP3330 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: One of the best professors in the department.  Notes are online, doesn't care if you go to class, hard tests but he curves a lot.  Study for the tests and you will love him.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: COP3503 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: He explains things very well, and gives good examples. He is very helpful too, and answers emails quickly. Also gives you the needed code for your programs.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CS13PROGL | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: HANDS DOWN ONE OF THE MOST HELPFUL AND GENUINE PROFESSORS AT UCF. WE NEED MORE LIKE HIM. TAKE AS MANY CLASSES WITH HIM AS YOU CAN - HE REALLY CARES AND TEACHES WELL AND EVEN SPEAKS ENGLISH.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: COP3502C | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Nice guy. He reads the powerpoints in class. If you ask a question he will do his best to explain it several ways. Good teacher. He also puts a massive curve on his tests - they are however incredibly difficult.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: MANYCGS | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Very monotone, however, very fair.  In each of the classes I had him as a teacher for, he would curve.  So there was some hope.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: COP3330 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: He read from notes and can be monotone, but was readily available for extra help.  Tests were not easy, so study hard, and was good about weighing the grade based on class performance.  Good professor to take.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: COP3330 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Very good teacher, but it is boring. Always there to help. Curves and reweights grades. Very Resonable. Taking him for CS1 in the spring.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: COP3330 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Very good teacher of a boring joke class.  Tests and prog.'s were simple compared to other CS classes, but what do you expect from a class that's mostly for IT people. :) But overall, one of the best in the department!

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: COP3330 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Tries to explain concepts in different ways to make sure you get it. Tests were difficult so study hard, programming assignments were easy if time consuming. Book was an expensive paperweight, all you need for reference is on the Java website.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: COP3102 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Taught Computer Science 2 a while back. He was very straight forward and clear. His tests are challenging but fair if you stayed awake during class and took good notes. As you all know, you can do a lot worse from other professors.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: VARIOUSCS | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Not that great of a teacher, but better than some of his cohorts.  All he does is read you his notes...which just happen to be large text excerpts from the book.  That's a horrible way to teach.  He also isn't very helpful one on one.  I learned nothing..

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: COP3330 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Teaches a little backwards, just reads his notes from the board, gives you a test, then give you the assignment that was based from the last test that you took.  If you want a nap time this is the class to take, not worth going to class.

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: VARIOUSCS | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: He got me through all of the entry level CS classes with no problem.. very easygoing and straightforward for CS1, CS2, Databases, etc..
Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: COP | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: No Comments

Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CGS3245 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: somebody said poor notes? HA! his notes are the most meticulous i've ever seen. yea his class is boring, but CS/databases/etc aren't supposed to be some sort of wild circus. he definitely knows what he's talking about and is very understanding.
Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: COP3602H | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: He was so easy to understand! the best programing class I have ever had
Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: COP3503 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Material should be presented better, poor notes.
Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CGS2545 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: hw class and tests are hell, lab is easy. find a new teacher if able
Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CGS2545 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Not a bad teacher, the PPT slides get old though. Tests are pretty hard!
Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: COP3503 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Great teacher, almost no studying needed.
Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CGS2545 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: awesome teacher, hard tho
Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: CGS2545 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Made an easy course difficult.
Professor: Mark Llewellyn | Course: COP3503 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Great teacher. Very knowledgeable of subject matter. "A+"

Professor: Mark Heinrich | Course: EGN4630 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A | Review: Very caring professor. Class essentially boils down to a weekly meeting with the professor and a weekly PowerPoint presentation. The lectures can be a bit dry, but the class is a free A as long as you show up to present (Grades are given by peer evaluation from your group). Would highly recommend taking this course as a tech elective.

Professor: Mark Heinrich | Course: EGN4630 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: The FAQ on the application website explains it well. There's a weekly team PPT presentation. You are expected to take the time to schedule and attend MANY meetings/ interviews. I would not recommend taking this class if you have other difficult or time-intensive classes in the semester. However, it's an interesting and rewarding class to take.

Professor: Mark Heinrich | Course: CDA | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: EGN4630. Dr Heinrich wants everyone to succeed & is very personable. He met with each group individually each week to discuss expectations, research, etc. Seemed very lenient w/ grading & presentation format, & only one small graded assignment outside of final presentation/video. The course can be stressful & class runs long, but otherwise great!

Professor: Mark Heinrich | Course: CDA | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: EGN4641C-Engineering Entrepreneurship. 
This class is one of those classes where you actually learn a lot. Pick this class if you are interested in learning about entrepreneurship, but do expect this to take a lot of your time. The professor is very passionate about this and will stay past time to provide insightful feedback.

Professor: Mark Heinrich | Course: CDA | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: EGN4641. Class was easy A and prof is very nice. My only issue is that he would go over EVERY TIME. Class ended at 3:50pm but he would go over at least 15 minutes every class. Once we had to stay until 4:20pm, 30 min after class ended. So be aware of that

Professor: Mark Heinrich | Course: COP4934 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: Dr. Heinrich is great! UCF's Senior Design is a very hard course (split across 2 semesters). The overarching goal of the SD1 course is a 150+ page paper, which is written from about the 3rd week in to 11:59pm the day it is due. The paper is very difficult, but very representative of the real CS world. Every school should have this course.

Professor: Mark Heinrich | Course: COP4935 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: Like SD1 before it, very little interaction with professor. Even less so, since there's no quizzes and only a few lecture days. Heinrich offers useful feedback during the 2 presentations/demos you'll do before the final demo that determines whether you'll graduate on time. Keep your group on task!

Professor: Mark Heinrich | Course: COP4934 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: Ultimately this class is entirely determined by the project. What I want to mention is that Heinrich WILL NOT grade your assignments on time, but he does tend to be pretty nice when grading. Leinecker is faster but far more nit picky from what I've seen. His lectures are interesting, and attendance is usually required but wasn't this year due.

Professor: Mark Heinrich | Course: COP4934 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: Your experience in this class is largely going to depend on what project you do and who's in your group. That said, Heinrich is a funny guy, and the lectures he gave were enjoyable. They weren't related directly to the project, more so on things to know once you graduate. The class itself has a few quizzes, and one overarching document to write.

Professor: Mark Heinrich | Course: COP4934 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: Heinrich is the kind of guy I'd love to get a beer with. He's a professor that actually accomplished something in life (unlike most) and teaches because he enjoys helping his students become successful professionals. He knows human nature, and structures group projects so that everyone is accountable for their work.

Professor: Mark Heinrich | Course: COP4935 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: A | Review: Dr. Heinrich is Amazing, I took him for senior design and he literally ensured my team's success by helping us work together as a team. He gave us crucial design advice and made our project easier and more efficient. I would highly recommend him for CS senior design.
Professor: Mark Heinrich | Course: CDA5106 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Really awesome teaching! loves to help students very much !

Professor: Mark Heinrich | Course: CDA5106 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: B+ | Review: Excellent professor. A stanford PhD in CS. He explains concepts like a comedian. You will love it. One of the best professors that you can meet in your life, that too if you are lucky. Tests are open book, open everything. Gives better grades than deserved. You will learn a lot. He co-founded two companies and inspires students to do the same.
Professor: Mark Heinrich | Course: CDA5106 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: AWESOME!!!

Professor: Mark Heinrich | Course: CDA5106 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: B | Review: AMAZING professor and person. Class is challenging and he expects a lot from you, but this is a good thing to learn the material inside and out. Expects you to know a lot of information, but will help you though it...  but he is doing it for your benefit. He really cares about his students and will go the extra mile to help them. Great guy.

Professor: Mark Heinrich | Course: CDA3103 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Take this guy for any and all architecture classes that you need and he offers. Brilliant guy, easy to understand, and sometimes it feels like he's a standup comedian (keeps a terribly boring subject interesting). You'll learn a lot (cache use is important so listen up software people!) Listen for hints about tests, and expect to have to think.

Professor: Mark Heinrich | Course: CDA3103 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Very Helpful professor. Go to his office hours as he is very eager to help students.  Very knowledgeable in his field, and teaches coherently.  Book reading recommended.. as his lecturing may go over your head a few times.

Professor: Mark Heinrich | Course: CDA5106 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Great teacher, very nice and helpful. He curves tests. Content is hard since I didn't have a good background. I had to read the books and study a lot. Worth it if you can record his lectures since he speaks fast. Overall good class and learned a lot. Great teacher. Be prepared to study on your own and do your homework

Professor: Mark Heinrich | Course: CDA3103 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: First half was really interesting (logic design and ASM programming) which is also the useful half (I doubt I will ever do IEEE754 arithmetic or cache mapping by hand again). The final project was a lot easier than it sounds. Still managed to make it as interesting as possible.

Professor: Mark Heinrich | Course: CDA3103 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: My favorite lecturer at UCF so far...keeps the topic as interesting as possible and kinda fun, but still transfers knowledge.  Take notes!  They will save you later.  I referenced the textbook for homework, but could have gotten away with google.  Tests are moderately difficult, but not crazy.  Tests for understanding, not regurgitation.

Professor: Mark Heinrich | Course: CDA4150 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: Although the tests were open book, I got high 80s on the tests without the book.  I got about 50% on every HW(which are really hard to figure out what to do), but still got an A since he curves a lot.  Final project is HARD.

Professor: Mark Heinrich | Course: CDA5106 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Very knowledgeable about computer architecture and helpful, easy to understand, and overall good lecturer. Challenging class- majority of grade (70%) was decided by final project and final exam, which tends to lead to a lot of end-of-semester stress. Still learned a lot, and would take a class from him again.

Professor: Mark Heinrich | Course: CDA4150 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Amazing teacher. Answers emails at all hours of the night usually within minutes of it being sent. Extremely willing to clarify and help you understand the material but there are horribly difficult Verilog assignments. Great teacher, EXTREMELY hard class.
Professor: Mark Heinrich | Course: CDA4150 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Tough class, but good professor who knows his topic.  A credit to the department imo.
Professor: Mark Heinrich | Course: CDA | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Horrible. He does not care at all about the class.
Professor: Mark Heinrich | Course: CDA | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: no good

Professor: Mark Heinrich | Course: CDA4150 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Very difficult class.  High-level and lots of time to put on it.  Projects are extra-hard and make 50% of grade.  You will learn a lot, but plan on investing 2 or 3 times more time than any other class.  Good class handouts and tests are open-book.  Nobody finished mid-term.  Learn Verilog and MIPS

Professor: Mark Heinrich | Course: CDA4150 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Expected it to be hard (4000 level), and it is, but prof is fun and makes things interesting. More practical knowledge than theory, which is good. Gives plenty of help whenever asked. Definitely recommend if you are serious about learning.

Professor: Mark Heinrich | Course: CDA4150 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: His need to prove how bright he is gets in the way of his actually teaching his students. Test averages as low as 45. Little instruction is given on how to do project. He prefers you to go to his off and ask questions rather than explain it properly in class. Run from this class!!!!

Professor: Mark Heinrich | Course: CDA6107 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Lots of work outside class, but HW reinforces material. Midterm & Final very hard, with class avgs in high 60's. 90% of "comprehensive" Final on last few classes only (reviewed A LOT just for the "learning experience"!). VERY accessible!! Night owl.

Professor: Mark Heinrich | Course: CDA4150 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: If you want to learn way more than you need to, he is the way to go.  The class was extremely difficult, and way too time consuming.  Final grade was curved tremedously though.
Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: C- | Review: needs to retire already

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: He gives certain exams online and certain exams in person depending on his mood. He barely communicates in a timely order and puts all responsibility on you despite doing everything to make success as difficult as possible.
Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: F | Review: Montagne tornado!!!!
Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: Montagne and Jahani need to collab.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: the content itself wasn't too bad but half the time he didnt even know what was going on and on top of that he is a bad grader. He will literally go "this is wrong - 0 points" like thank YOU that is so helpful.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Drop/Withdrawal | Review: Students literally have to correct the professor numerous times + harshly AND usually incorrectly grades your exam questions. TAs never know what he's teaching because it's not in any known textbook and cannot help you. Shown up over 15 minutes late to his own class 10 times now.
Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: Dude is a prime example that some people are too old to be working.
Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Incomplete | Review: I am glad this professor is retiring.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3269 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: C | Review: Does not care anymore and needs to retire. completely unhelpful with questions and does not even respond to emails.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3269 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: C | Review: Awful communicator and changes his mind about the course every day, very unclear grading criteria and just overall a poor professor you make one mistake in this class and there goes a good grade, it's time to put the fries in the bag Monty
Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: C | Review: horrible canvas layout, do not take under any circumstances.
Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: C | Review: Needs to retire. Its that time. Tenure is over.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: C | Review: Seriously If you don't attend lectures you might as well fail the class. This professor gives you all the answers to the tests, and homework. His teaching might be a little off for some people but you just have to adapt. Listen carefully. 10/10 professor, Very caring.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: Unfortunately, I think it is time for him to retire. He knows what he's talking about, but has difficulty teaching it. Tests are graded by multiple TAs at once, so grades are very inconsistent. This class is harder than it has to be because of the TAs and the professor.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: Monty is pretty nice when you ask for help. My issue is that the exams are graded poorly. the ta's graded the majority of the questions one by one. Their reasons for taking off points would be random and inconsistent for each student. I do think monty should retire soon and let another professor hold the reins for this class.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CDA3103 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: B | Review: He made lectures fun and there were only a few coding assignments throughout the course. There's a midterm and a final exam. The lack of assignments makes it easy to accidentally slack off, so make sure to keep up with the content.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: B | Review: Prepare for partner work & get projects started early and SHOW UP TO LECTURES!  He has great review days yes, but the lectures are also helpful for projects, reiterated points / exact worded definitions for tests, and for answering questions to test your attendance!  Very informative, makes the topic interesting!  Honesty great compared to McAlpin.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: D | Review: Montagne wants you to fail. When polled, 42% of respondent students lost 15% of their grade due to miscommunication, which Montagne was too egotistical to fix. Also, the final exam median was a 41; one student was punished for "P1 moves to ready on I/O interrupt" instead of saying "The OS moves P1 to ready on I/O interrupt."

He will fail you.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: C- | Review: The professor failed to address his students' concerns properly. He would give vague answers to vital questions raised in class. His attitude during lectures was standoffish and not conducive to a productive environment. It seemed, more often than not, that he was more inclined to induce his students to fail than to guide them toward success.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: Most of the course is a project split into 4 assignments, each worth 15% of your grade. Rubric will change a mere 1-2 days before it is due which will contradict the initial guide. Harsh grading results in going from a passing grade to unable to pass from one single assignment if you mess up. Lectures have  no direction and barely help. Avoid.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP4910 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: The ratings do not paint Prof. Montagne in a good light but he is very good at teaching this course specifically. Assignments are give 3 presentations and write a report. He can be critical of your work but if you listen to his feedback and prepare for your presentations you will be fine. The class is very hard to fail, he wants you to succeed.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: A | Review: Don't let my grade fool you, this class was insanely difficult. His lectures were extremely boring. I only passed because of self-teaching and fellow peers. Recording his lectures is heavily heavily recommended

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: B | Review: This is the worst professor I have ever taken. He is routinely 15 minutes late to class. Class is graded mostly by four hw's. They are long and the requirements often are contradicting or are changed TWO days before its due. Unprofessional and lazy. Gives terrible feedback on assignments. TA's are borderline useless. Avoid this class at all costs.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: Montagne is a professor you take because you have to, not because you can. In my case for Computer Architecture Concepts, he was the only option and that is the only context I will ever pick him under. While I can see that he does care and wants his students to succeed I think he lacks all required skills or talent to make that happen.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: if I could give him a 0 I would. Avoid his class at all costs...he was a menace all semester and this class literally gave me so much anxiety. Made exams online last minute and gave us a penalty of 10% each minute we submitted our exam late. Not helpful at all and his lectures were useless. If you can self teach you're set,if not... you're cooked.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: Record lectures. Exams are hopeless without recordings. Lectures often inaccurate and confusing. Grading is inconsistent among identical student responses and points are lost even if you answer right and fully. Short time for exams, most do not earn a 70. Asked to buy a textbook you won't ever touch. Grades take a long time to come in. 1-on-1s OK.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CDA3103 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: B | Review: The most disorganized class that I've ever been apart of. There was a heavy emphasis on the grades that you got on the few assignments: 3 homework assignments, 1 group project, the midterm, and the final. I'd say the only way to pass is to self study as his teaching was subpar at best.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CDA3103 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: C | Review: Really caring guy, but this class is so disorganized. Attendance isn't mandatory for lectures, but good luck trying to do any of the assignments or exams if you are not attending. Also recommend going to his office hours, it helps a lot.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: I can accept a professor who requires attending class to get the information required to pass. I can accept a professor who has a grating lecture style where they spend 10 minutes asking the class for a hyperspecific definition instead of just saying it. I cannot accept both at once.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CDA3103 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: B- | Review: Worst professor I've ever had at UCF, hands down. His lectures are very disorganized and just straight up wrong at some points. The exams are not terrible, but the grading is incredibly strict and not forgiving at all for partial points. Avoid taking this professor at ALL COSTS!!!

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: First time I've ever felt compelled to review a professor. His grading rubrics contradict themselves, lectures are unhelpful, and the rubrics aren't updated. During the final review he stopped mid-sentence, shrugged, and left 30 minutes early. Worst prof I've had; I'd retake Guhas discrete class every semester before sitting through another of his.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CDA3103 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: Class is very disorganized, and he barely teaches anything. Keeps going over the same few things over and over. Exam questions are easy, but if your answer is not perfect you lose most of the points. The lab TA is absolutely horrendous and is impossible to listen to with how monotone her speech is. Take if you don't care about learning.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: Lacks consistency when grading, really poorly organized class. You can copy 1:1 from his lecture to a test and he will mark it wrong, it makes no sense. Insanely hard to reach, even through his recommended way of contact. Complains no one comes to class (attendance is optional??). Homework's all online, but kind of useless. Exams were all online.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: B | Review: Online midterm had an average and median of around 50 each. Assignment specifications are vague and confusing, and understanding him in class requires you to already know the content and decypher through his catastrophic elocution. Avoid him at all costs.

Else, attend all labs, get started early on hw, study slide examples, read his book.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3269 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: This professor lacks structure in Webcourses, making it hard to find info. They have a mean attitude, are difficult to reach, and are very strict on grading. They also struggle to properly implement the grading scheme, causing confusion. The class felt disorganized and frustrating—take it with someone else if possible.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: R U N. I am so serious. Like take this class somewhere else if possible, he is the most inconsiderate professor I've ever dealt with, like ever.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: C+ | Review: Attendance is not mandatory, BUT highly recommended (otherwise you will be lost)(try to go at least to the exam reviews). We had 3 homework assignments (DONT MISS ANY) and 3 exams (All of them were online for us). I dont think he deserves all the bad ratings his gets tbh, This is supposed to be the hardest course for IT, and he made it manageable

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Wasnt too bad of a professor. Lecture heavy try not to miss. All test online including final three in total. Three homework programming assignments in Assembly not too hard they basically build on top of each other. Honestly would take again.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: important to attend lectures since he doesn't post his notes; only slides. assignments are straightforward, and most of the time he would write the exact answer in a previous lecture. exams weren't terrible, but they were lengthy. drawing a lot of the diagrams takes a ton of time and his exams are full of them. he's very helpful in office hours.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: B | Review: Montagne is good at presenting material in a basic overview. However, I watched him belittle students many times just for asking him to reiterate (he tends to make mistakes teaching and will waste 30 minutes starting anew)

Test questions are vague but expect complex answers that aren't mentioned. Most TA's are sticklers to the rubric (theres none)

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: C | Review: Do not skip class. It's literally an auto fail course if you skip class. Go to the exam reviews at LEAST bc he gives questions and answers that are literally on the test at reviews. Don't chegg it'll be a flat 0. He's much nicer and understanding in office hours. The TAs are horrible graders, literally even worse than him. Good luck taking him

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: C | Review: Will call you out in class. Cannot understand him well. Will never respond to emails. Tests were online, but never gave confirmation of that till a few days before the exam.    Had 3 ( was going to be 4, but hurricane ) coding assignments that make up 40% of the grade. 2 Midterms, 15% each. And the final making 30% of the overall grade.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: D | Review: 3,000 total lines of C, 1,200 lines 20h+ final project 0% because "it wasn't structured correctly"

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: D | Review: Got a 97% on the final understood all the class material by the end, however ended with a D due to messing up the first part of the compiler project. Wasn't able to understand 50% of what he Lectured and he is extremely stubborn on the definitions that are not entirely what's reflected in the real world.

 Avoid for systems software.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: B+ | Review: Prof Montagne has a specific teaching style, but engaging with it can pay off. Attend lectures, read the free textbook, and use resources like unstuckstudy ai to prep - you'll learn a lot. Grading can be harsh, so stay on top of projects and exams.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: Prof is excellent lecturer & very kind. Tough grader. Must attend lectures if you want to succeed, this class is not for lazy people. You will learn a lot from doing the project. TAs were terrible, they were very clearly confused about the project rubric and would make many last minute changes. Most likely miscommunication between the prof and TAs.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: B+ | Review: Dr. Montagne is a good lecturer. However, the programming project felt disorganized and difficult to follow at times. This difficulty is not due to actual rigor, but unclear criteria and often changed requirements close to the due date. Because the project is 60% of your final grade, I feel like the details should be clear the first time.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: B+ | Review: He is a very nice professor. You need to go to classes, he gives hints and explains projects during class. Homeworks are toughly graded but as long as you read the rubric you will get an A on them, just start early. Midterms are online. He says what will be on them in class.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A- | Review: Professor Montagne's in-person lectures are an absolute must attend or you will fail the class. Exam content is straight from the notes that are taken in person. It's graded by 3 exams and 4 assignments so you gotta go to office hours if you have any questions or just to confirm that your work is good.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: Coding assignments aren't as hard as ppl make them seem; he gives you pseudocode to follow. If you start those when they are assigned you should have no problem getting ~90%. Do well on these as the coding projects are 60% of your grade. Tests are REALLY easy as he gives you most of the answers during the test review beforehand.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: C | Review: Montagne is very difficult to understand during lectures. When it comes to projects, instructions are unclear and difficult to understand, along with having strict grading. The exams are extremely difficult and the grading is very particular to the slides. The content is pretty straightforward, but this class is unfairly difficult.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: The material itself is not hard. He grades very harshly on exams. The Homework is even worse. The TA's grading is inconsistent and lazy. The professor told us the day before the first exam whether it would be online or in person, & 10 minutes before, how we are supposed to submit it. For someone teaching such a lot time it seems he has given up.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Drop/Withdrawal | Review: I never wore a seatbelt on my way to this class because I wanted to die. I've had a handful of bad professors but I've never had one so bad that I felt inclined to leave a bad rating on this site to warn others, until now. Lectures are just him reading off of slides and rubrics/grading for homework assignments are extremely strict.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: Incomplete | Review: Exams and assignments are a joke. The material is very easy, but the things we get graded on are very very complicated and specific. For the exam, we had absolutely no time to do it and I was 30 seconds late to submit and he is taking off 20% for that. He is unforgiving grader and person.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: C | Review: Confusing lectures however the worst part of the course was the assignment grading. The rubrics for the assignments did not line up to the grade given whatsoever. Additionally I was graded incorrectly for an assignment and that wasn't fixed until the last week of the semester.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: D | Review: This class made me want to quit pursuing IT because he is the only professor teaching this course at UCF, completely awful. Rarely referenced the textbook and his teaching method was making a bunch of scribbles on paper projected on the screen. When asked for clarification, he would answer the same way and move on. TAs were awful graders. Avoid!!!!

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: C | Review: 3 projects/hw worth a total of 40% of your grade. 2 exams with 15% each and the final was 30%. He wouldn't let us know the format of the exam usually until the day before. His TA is the only reason the class was possible. The practice exams provided had conflicting answers from what was taught in class but were usually still close to the exam.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: B | Review: Very poor lecturer and his way of teaching this certain subject was completely WRONG compared to other teachers and authors online/youtube. Will literally ask the class if the example is right even though it is your first time learning it. Guaranteed, none of the stuff you learn here except the 7 segment display will be applied to in your career.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: D+ | Review: worst professor i have ever taken avoid at ALL costs. i received a 0 on a project that took hours of time because there were a few errors in the output. the ta that gave the grade also decided to regrade the project before it one and changed it from a 95 to a 50. i had a high b going into finals week and out of nowhere i failed. extremely unfair.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CAP4314 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: B | Review: Such a headache to deal with. Unclear assignment criteria. ALWAYS 5-15 MINUTES LATE TO HIS OWN CLASSES. Avoid if at all possible.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: C | Review: Horrible professor, actively accused my team of cheating in the project (we didn't) and had to wait until the end to get it regraded by a TA, I would not take him for any other classes he offers in the future, truly a bad experience, despite the topics for SS being interesting; if you have the misfortune of having to take him, focus on the project.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: B | Review: I also took him for computer and design architecture and it has been awful both times. In this course, he will change grading criteria on a whim and is an extremely harsh grader on the exams. He will also not tell you if the exam is online or not until the day before. Will never take a class with him again if I can avoid it

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: B | Review: He frequently makes errors during his lectures, and tends to lack organization. Expect lectures that are not very clear, assignment instructions that are not detailed, and study materials for exams that are quite ambiguous.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3269 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: He's not great but it's definitely doable. Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP4600 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: The course material is incredibly outdated and does not apply to modern systems. Is child-like nitpicky about definitions in exams and has no problem giving 0 credit for answers if a synonym is used instead of an exact definition. Final exam questions had very little to do with what was actually covered in class. DO NOT take this class.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: If you have another option for System Software, take the other professor. Class consist of 4 Hws and two test. First two HWs are jokes, he practically gives you all the pseudo code you need. HWs 3 and 4 are a big jump in terms of difficulty, both having an average around 60. Both test were poorly composed, it felt like I was making the test.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: B+ | Review: He only wants to give his lectures and doesn't care about the administrative part of the class, his TAs do all the grading. There are 4 assignments, 1/2 were easy, 3/4 were insanely hard and lengthy. His instructions on the hw were VERY vague. I got two power-hungry undergrad TAs who were strict on the grading despite no grading criteria given.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3673 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: D | Review: Worst professor I've ever taken at UCF hands down. This guy is a clown and has no sympathy for students. Grades extremely hard on exams and the reviews are verbal meaning there is no clarification whatsoever on what is going to be on any of the exams. Over 40% of students got a 0 on the first assignment which lead most of them to fail the class.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3763 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Way too picky on exams. Wrote dispatch instead of dispatcher on exam 1 and got a 0/10 on it instead of half credit. Dude is way too picky for his own good. If you aren't good at memorizing random information try to avoid at all cost. Assignments are pretty easy however.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: Read slides that he doesn't even prepare. Homeworks are poorly explained and he does not give you good resources to learn the material.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3763 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: He really cares that his students understand and learn the material. Ask questions if you're lost. There are 3 assignments and 3 exams. There is always one or two question(s) in the exam about the assignments. Go to his office hours!

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: B | Review: Honestly I only went to class maybe 5 times. The test material is dense but doable. Study early and get ready to memorize. I failed the first test but pulled through and got a 100 on the final. Professor is a good guy, but a tough grader. If you commit to the class you will be fine.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3763 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: My brotha Montagne is probably the best professor at UCF. He gives that real feedback that gets you through the entire course ez pz. It helped a lot to be on the discord server though. In which, my name was Nut, and we got mega LIT in that server bruh lmao. The 3 assignments are hard af but as long as you follow along it will be alright my dude.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3763 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: Passing this class isn't hard, you just have to actually show up and do the homework. I noticed that alot of the students who complained, like this one guy whose discord name was Yami, didn't even bother to show up to class. Goes the extra mile to help his students.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3763 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: B | Review: Montagne is a caring professor who tries to do what he can for you to understand concepts. The class itself is slightly difficult, especially the last coding assignment using the C-- assembly language. Tests are generally straightforward since he mentions possible topics. Just to remember to load and eh.. store and you get no minos marks.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: I took Professor Montagne for computer architecture, and was scared at first because of previous reviews. However, after taking my final today I can truthfully say I am happy to have prof montagne. Montagne is an amazing professor, material gone over in class is just the exams. 3 exams, 3 homeworks. Finished the class with a 98.4. Great class.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3763 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: The course was really boring and hard to maintain interest, I learned a lot of my material from youtube. But Professor Montagne is a really good and caring professor. He wants all his students to succeed and if you come to him for help... you'll get that and so much more! Exams were cake, but the programs were RIDICULOUSLY hard. Study on youtube.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3763 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: His tests are hard if you take his class on campus. They are devided into 2 separate testing days (for example: half on Wednesday and the other half on Friday). It is a lot of memorization. Be prepared to learn a new programming language.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: C | Review: Class had one one exam and a project divided into 4 homeworks. First two were easy, last 2 were too hard. No grading criteria, you just hope for a TA that has a soul. People whose programs would not even compile got same grades as people who had one error. Exam 1 average was 54% and he doesn't curve at all. Final was about 75%. Almost half failed.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3763 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: F | Review: the class was not hard over all. what made it hard was the homeworks he assigned and downloading the ide for the hw. His test are exactly what he says. Programming projects are HARD. Do not miss class because he at times does board work instead of powerpoints. He knows his stuff. His TA are the worst and will take points off everything. emails good

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: Very helpful professor and is good at teaching concepts. Only issue is he has an accent which can be bad in bigger rooms, but once you get used to it it's fine. He's a good professor.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: The content in this class is not the easiest to grasp, but that's not what makes this class hard. The majority of the grade is created by projects that are explained insufficiently with no test cases provided until hours before it is due. The other part of the grade is two exams that are long answer responses. The projects make you want to cut.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: B+ | Review: Take him again? You don't really have a choice. He's the only professor that teaches Comp Arch or OS Concepts. He's not terrible, but very vague in his instructions and grades pretty touch. The good thing is that when you turn in your test he'll tell you if you're missing a certain thing so he cares about you passing, just put in some effort.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: B | Review: Definitely attend class or you won't be able to keep up. He doesn't post lectures online, but he does post stuff from the text. 3 tests, 3 HW's. The first two HW's were easy, but the last one was pretty difficult. This class is basically math (binary, octal, hex, dec) and drawing. His exam reviews were helpful and don't be afraid to ask questions.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3763 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: C | Review: Good professor, though he is very strict about definitions on exam questions. Also has a bit of an accent, so it might be better to sit near the front.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: C | Review: He tells you that you don't need to recite definitions exactly but gives you no points on tests if your definition is not exact. Requires you to memorize entire programs and copy down the code for tests. Most people in my class averaged with a C or D on all tests. Wouldn't recommend him.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: B | Review: Definitely not an easy class. I know a ton of people who have had to retake it to pass. You have to teach yourself the material and study a lot. Lectures werent great. Also not the worst class Ive taken.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3763 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Had 3 test and 3 assignments. Very clear grading and clear guidelines given at the beginning of the class. He reviews what will be exactly on the test the class before the test. He is a really cool professor that will totally help you out if you just ask.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: B | Review: Montagne is an awful teacher. He's unclear in his lectures and even worse in his homework and test questions. Multiple times, what he said in class contradicted the official homework description he gave us. His tests are confusing and poorly worded. He never covered everything that was on the test. If you can take this class with anyone else, do so

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3763 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: Professor Montagne is a very caring professor and will help you a lot in his office hours. There are three exams and three HW assignments. The HW assignments can be hard but I would recommend starting early and go to his office hours. The exams are a bit easier and if you study his study guides, you will get a good grade.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: Nice guy that really meant well, but not organized well; projects difficult but graded easily. Likes to draw messy drawings on his whiteboard. Project was to make a compiler (split into 4 parts). You get one partner and can't change that person (you choose). Make sure you're at the class before the exam because he tells you what's gonna be on it.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: C+ | Review: Lectures consist of reading off slides and selecting random students to answer questions that haven't been gone over yet. The slide shows are inconsistent, and share the same broken English that Montagne speaks in. You will find yourself misinformed for tests and assignments, but expected to get answers exactly right. Avoid if you want to learn.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3763 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: By far one of my favorite professors at UCF. I took two classes with this professor and wish i could take more. If you take the time to understand the content you will learn this material and pass the class. The homework is challenging but helpful. The professor will go out of his way to help you succeed in this course.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Please see Dr. Montagne in office hours! He is amazingly helpful in office hours. See him early if you are struggling! The project is to build a compiler. The exams usually ask for drawn diagrams from the slides. Try to pay attention in class. Otherwise, why bother coming to class? This is one of the hardest classes at UCF.  Beware of the midterm.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: Communication was the biggest issue with this professor. The programming project was really cool but he gave terrible instructions. There was little clarity on what was expected. I had no idea what was going to be on the test. I found someone who had taken the class with this professor before so I was able to kind of figure out what to study.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: C | Review: AVOID Euripides Montagne!!! I would not wish him on my worst enemy. Comes to class late practically every lecture. Can't give a clear description of assignment requirements, providing students with incorrect/old code examples. Extremely harsh grader. If you do end up with him, pray you have good TA's because thats what saved me when I took it.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: C+ | Review: Terrible. Material is read 85% from slides and the other 15% consists of him drawing illegible diagrams that are sometimes wrong. Homework requirements are extremely ambiguous. The code he gave us to use with our homework had all kinds of errors and memory leaks that the students then had to fix. Rubrics got changed mid-assignment with no notice.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: C+ | Review: He teaches very difficult abstract material. That is a challenge for anyone to teach to fresh minds. Having said that he really tries to help you understand. He is very accessible outside of class and is happy to see and help students. Again, the subject matter is very tough so don't mistake his rough grades for poor instruction.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: Montaigne knows his material and explains it well.  He can be hard to understand at times and will sometimes be confused by questions though.  He is a great professor and gives clear guidance on all programs.  He also has a tendency to help with programs in class to the point where you are basically just translating his notes in code.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3763 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: B | Review: Professor Montagne is one of the most genuine professors at UCF.  He really cares about your grade and understanding of the material.  I thought his lectures were very information heavy, but he always reviewed well before exams and aimed to help you out with programming assignments.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Major train wreck. Professor and TA's don't agree on anything and refer you back and forth if you ask a question. Project guidelines are totally ambiguous and contradictory, as are expectations. TA's take forever to grade assignments, which is a source of major frustration since they are interdependent. Very unorganized and an overall mess. Avoid!

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: B | Review: Lectures are confusing. You are better off trying to decipher the powerpoints at home.  The assignment documents are inconsistent and full of errors.  When you ask him for clarifications he refers you to the TAs, then the TAs refer you back to him.  You go into exams without practice material on which to apply the concepts.  Take someone else.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: He does not post notes from lectures, so attend class! If you go to class, you will ace all of his exams or at the very least pass them. His reviews are awesome and will prepare you for the exams. He's very helpful during office hours if you need help. Answers any questions or doubts you may have. Take him!

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP4910 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: Very easy class; four presentations on trending topics in IT plus one term paper. He offers you very specific pointers on what he wants to see in your presentations and paper. Follow those guidelines and you'll always get at least a B if you try. If you skip other students' presentations he docks your grade, so beware.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP4020 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: A nice guy. I recommend attending lectures and paying attention, even if they aren't that exciting. You will need to do some studying, but the reviews essentially covered what would be on the test. If he tells you to go home and read a paper, do it. The last HW was tough, but you can totally do it if you put your mind to it. Got an A.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3763 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: B+ | Review: Gives a couple of programs which he basically guides you on how to do. Tests have review sessions the class before. Listen to the reviews and you will pass your tests easily. He is specific with wording on tests but it makes sense. Do your HW & Tests well and you will pass. Don't really need to show up for anything besides the review and test days.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3763 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: His assignments are not that difficult, but they do require work. He gives you step by step instructions on how to do each assignment, it all depends on whether you went to class. He's really nice if you go to his office hours and helpful. He gives exam reviews the day before and they are almost verbatim. Go to class, take solid notes and get an A.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3763 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: A | Review: The class material is ver difficult. If you fail assignments you most likely will fail the class since the HW are worth 25% of your final grade.   Lots of programming in a computer language that is not studied in class (BACI c--). if you can take it with someone else. not an easy class.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: B+ | Review: Great teacher, most of the class will be drawing and memorization. If you miss class you wont do good on the exams. His instructions on homework and tests are not clear sometimes but he is always there to help and answer questions. He is a tough grader, Homework weighs a lot on your grade for it is 20% of your grade and the rest are 3 tests.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3763 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: B | Review: There are only 3 tests and an unspecified amount of homework assignments so it's hard to recover from one bad grade. They aren't too difficult though. He likes to call on people randomly so know your stuff and always always know the definition of an operating system

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: When the semester began I was super nervous about there not being enough material to study outside of class. I typically have a hard time paying attention in class, so that was a problem, but he is actually pretty easy to follow. Just make sure to write everything down if you're like me. You will need to take notes. His exam reviews are really good

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3673 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Probably one of the better professors. Tough grader for exams but he tells you exactly what he wants. Attend class cause he'll state what questions will be on the hw/exams. Overall easy class.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP4910 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: This is a public speaking class. Topic are picked by you as long as they are about technology. They all follow a specific PPT format and should be about 12-15 mins long. Could be in pairs or by yourself. One paper is due at the end which covers one of the 4-5 topics presented. Very simple. Show up and participate and you will pass no problem.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: OSCONCEPTS | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: B | Review: Montague is a boss. I don't care what anyone says about the guy . I took him for OS and I cannot think of a more understanding professor I've had. Talk to him if you're ever in any trouble, and he will sort you out. He loves to call on people too, so pay attention. When exams come around (2per semester) He spends a whole class preparing you. Study.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3673 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: B | Review: Montagne is an awesome professor. He  walks through problems up on the overhead and board. All you have to do is show up. He is quite the troll as well.  Just be warned that he will call on students to answer questions for the entire class.
