Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3763 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: He repeats himself a lot, but the stuff he repeats (the definition of an OS, virtual memory, etc) is on the test, so it is to your benefit. If you are a quick learner and pick  up concepts on the first lecture you might get frustrated with the constant review, but his lectures are in depth and HW&Exams are simple mostly. Great experience.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3401 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Tells you whats going to be on the test -- to a degree. You still have to study the topics he suggests to study and be able to recite definitions verbatim. Also keeps class interesting by trolling the students every once-in-awhile. Enjoy the compiler!

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3763 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Professor Montagne is knowledgeable and a funny guy.  He loves to troll the students. Attendance isn't mandatory, but you'll end up missing important information.  His tests are tough, but if you pay attention, study, and do the homework, you'll do well.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CAP4104 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A- | Review: He's tough, but not an ogre.  I found that while he likes to joke around and troll in his other classes, he was very serious in this class.  Two group presentations. I enjoyed thinking about interfaces and GUIs.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3763 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Wait and Take Jerry Hensel more hands on and actually learning things used in the workplace. Unless your skilled and understand operating systems, this class will test your patience. At the end I was disappointed in the fact I spent money on this, just learning how to take a test with information that has no real world use.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3763 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: His classes get much deeper than I expected, but he repeats things that need to be repeated.  He answers any question until the class is satisfied.  Great professor

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: An expert instructor with a good ear for when the class is lost and needs more guidance. The topics were interesting and he was always up for answering any extra questions any one had. He seems to know so much about the material. The compiler was a lot of work, but he was lenient with people who struggled but showed genuine effort and improvement.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP4020 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A | Review: This was one of the easiest classes I have ever taken. Euripides was nice, and the topics were actually interesting. He gave the questions and answers prior to exams. Attend class and you will get an A.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A | Review: Going into this class all I heard was how many people had to retake it and how difficult his grading is. Seriously? He gives the answers to every test in advance, and I got an A on every one. So if you go to class and study the material he tells you to, you will be fine. He is a genuinely nice professor, perhaps a tad boring.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: B | Review: While professor Euripides has a reputation for how harsh he can be on grading, he honestly knows all the material by heart and can help you if your having trouble, I failed both of his first tests but thanks to him giving my a few points back and I think a curve at the end I made it out with a C.  Make sure you talk to him and don't bs your way.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: He's super nice and literally tells you what is going to be on the test. His tests are straight forward and slightly changed questions that he gives you during the review. The compiler is tough, and that's what breaks a lot of students. It can't be done in a day, and that's what killed a lot of people in my class.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: D+ | Review: WORSE TEACHER I have ever taken. Very Rude. Teacher grades tests and homework strictly. This teacher is paranoid about cheating...He falsely accused three students last semester and accused me this semester. For your OWN SANITY...DO NOT TAKE HIM!

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: At best he's an inconsistent grader, at worst he makes questions up. If ur looking for a course to 'c' ur way out if, take him. If you care about your grade, avoid him.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: Rather_Not_Say | Review: If you attend class and pay attention, you should get a high grade. He has you work problem out in class which was very helpful to me. He is also very willing to help you understand every concept if you ask.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: Go to class and don't miss any of them. This guy is awesome I had him for operating systems as well. I received an A- because I was texting to much and not paying attention to the lecture in the beginning. Only kids that failed were the ones who didn't show up

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3763 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: B+ | Review: This professor is amazing one of the best lecturers at UCF no doubt. Simple way to put it if you do not go to class you will fail. I got an A and I only missed one class. Yes you have to study but everything is straight forward no surprises he'll stop and explain if you ask questions. I'll take him for any class you'll learn alot

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CDA3103 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Incredibly nice Professor! Go talk to him during office hours and you can get a ton of points back on your exams. He's willing to work with you. He tells you EXACTLY what is going to be on the exam the class right before it.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: Wow thanks for listening. You're a wonderful audience. What a hot potato, turned into a french fry every time he said assembly language, but I got better. 9/3pi stored using ISA standard for float values. You'll learn that in this class. Wow, simply amazing. Cowbell and such. Also, Wikipedia what he doesn't teach and you'll do fine. Quite.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CDA3103 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Nice guy! Likes to joke around with class. Not always the best at explaining things, but he's fairly well organized. When he says something will be on the exam, it usually is. Gives very good exam reviews. To do well in this class requires you to attend every lecture and ask questions when you don't understand. If you keep quiet, you'll regret it!

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CDA3103 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Awful teacher. Emphasizes some topics in class and includes completely different ones on exams. 2 exams so far and the class average has been so low he has had to offer extra credit for the average to be in the 70's. Syllabus says exams are open book/notes but last exam he changed this day of to "open mind". Avoid this teacher at all costs.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Hardest CS class Ive taken (taken everything in 3000 level and down). Definitely a hard subject, and the compiler project is difficult to do, BUT he is an extremely nice professor, and wants you to actually learn. SUPER helpful in office hours, GO TO THEM! Tells you exactly what will be on each exam, great reviews! Trolls the class and is hilarious

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: What a great instructor. He loves the parsing/scanning/programming languages subjects and it reflects in his lectures. He's not easy, but if you make an effort, he will make all the effort to help you.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Avoid this professor. Doesn't teach for how he'll test. Grades harshly in all cases. Somewhat boring in lectures and gets annoyed in office hours. Reading the book helps but it's mostly slides. Coding in teams of 2. Coding assignment is very time consuming (will vary by skill). Where does building a compiler from scratch apply to the real world??

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3763 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: I actually liked taking his class. Attending class is pretty important since he'll will emphasize what's going to be on the test and he'll give better explanations than in the notes. The tests are very similar to the hw which is nice considering the scope of the class. He's kinda boring for the most part, but he likes to troll.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Everyone complains about the tests. Very picky about answers, little partial credit, expects definitions to be written verbatim. I failed the first test, but knowing what to expect after that, I got an A in the course without too much effort. Assignments were fun. He likes to joke around, sometimes to the point of trolling.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CAP4104 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Really slow grader, we just took the final and 60% of our grades had yet to be graded/posted, assignments from 6 and 8 weeks ago still do not have grades.  Super strict on wording, teaches a subject one way the entire semester and switches it up for the final.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP4020 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Worse grader I've ever had, hes an OK lecturer, but his methods for evaluating your progress are terrible. Avoid this teacher at all costs, one of the worst professors you could take any class with at UCF

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Montagne is very nice and cares, but his tests are brutal and he grades them really hard. The project for 3402 is really easy, but the tests will destroy you. Go to his office hours every chance you can. Have him give you previous tests or help you or SOMETHING. You will fail all his tests (like I did) otherwise.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP4600 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: today he graded papers in class.  the last meet before the final, no chance for office hours. when grading some things are very unfair due to his lack of grasping the english language.  also the project for cop4600 will take increasingly long. each part reveals errors in previos sections. also his algorithm's broken. its nearly impossible to do.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: This teacher expects everything to be verbatim. The tests were very hard, and non multiple choice. He gives NO partial credit for the non-multiple choice tests. I wouldn't suggest you take this teacher if you like your GPA. I recieved a D in COP 3402 and a B in Human Computer Interaction with him.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Tests are tricky but he (or the TA) grades very generously. Only 2 homework assignments and 3 tests. I made an 'A', but had to pay attention in class, especially the session before the test. The final was brutal, but overall I think he is a good instructor.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP4600 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Took him for Operating Systems. Tests are fair, but his grading is extremely particular, takes off points if your answer is not what he wants EXACTLY. The kicker is the Operating System project he has you do throughout the semester. Had me working constantly and worrying about failing all semester. Virtually impossible to get an A.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP4600 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Attendance is almost mandatory since he occasionally mentions questions that will be in the test. He makes you interested in class because tests are harshly graded and if you don't answer verbatim, you will fail.He sometimes gives examples that are wrong in class. He loves trolling. Project is VERY time consuming if you want to get a 100% on it.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Overall, an okay teacher. Explains things very well. However, doesn't give enough homework in order to practice or go over it before tests, and the TA for my class sucked. Also, every test is cumulative, even if he leads you believe otherwise.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP4020 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Very "different" teacher and definitely not one of my favorites. Class seemed more like a test of how much frustration you could handle rather than how much you know. Test questions have multiple interpretations and his explanation of what it was asking left everyone even more confused. Half the material comes from graduate thesis papers! Not cool.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP4020 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: If you haven't taken System Software with this guy then don't take any other class with him. He didn't give us grades for more than half of our assignments. He does not curve at all. He expects a lot from his students. He is a hard grader as well. Don't argue with him or you will loose points on other questions. Very knowledgeable though.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP4020 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Lectures bit more fun than average. On tests, definitions/keywords must must be exact as he lectured or else no partial credit.  No online notes. No textbook needed. Write down everything on review.  Classes with him are harder than average CS classes, but easier than Physics, Calculus, Discrete classes.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP4610 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Does not post relevant notes for test often leaving his brief mention in class as your only guide. Expects IT students to have the same programming background as CS students. Requires learning of unrelated content for test (Must learn Assembly code to answer a test question about memory paging). All test are 2 days long 10 question short response.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP4610 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Had him for OS Lab. I learned more in this class than any other at UCF. He wasn't easy but utilized every minute of class time effectively. If you take good notes and memorize definitions, you'll be good. If you pay attention during class he subtlety lets you know some of the questions that will be on the exams. I'm an IT major and got an A.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Amazing professor, I learned a lot from his class. He gave us 5 programs this time around, the last 3 were insanely hard, but he lets you resubmit. Kinda boring if you don't interact w/ him during class. Ask questions! All in all, go to class, take mental notes of all diagrams, and memorize all definitions word by word and you'll do good.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Love this class! It is hard, but is really funny, and willing to bend over backwards to help students-- all you have to do is ask. Assignments are fair, but he does push the limits of your knowledge-- be prepared to study hard.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP2500 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: He is a good teacher though very hard to understand. Has good concepts. I highly recommend going to his office hours. He is very understanding and interested in your learning. Test are very tough. Don't miss class and pay attention to reviews and you will do fine. He loves when his students correct him.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP4020 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: One of the funniest teachers I've had & I'm about to graduate.One thing you need to understand about him is that in his class he is a King and you know NOTHING! Accept that fact & you'll do fine.Definitions & codes have to be done EXACTLY the way he wants it to be,not something similar.He grades you on what u DONT know rather than what you know!

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Great teacher! SUPER strict on test grading. MEMORIZE definitions verbatum. YOU cannot miss class but if you show up and ask questions you will pass. Chooose not to work in a team on projects like I did and you will grasp the material better but may take longer programming.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Very strict grader.  Pay attention to EXACT words he uses to describe concepts.  He will expect that same definition word-by-word on tests.  Compiler assignment is tough, but doable.  Just make sure your code for each assignment works properly, since they build on each other.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP2500 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Got an A, but this guy sucks. If you ask a question he'll just repeat what he said. After every test, I also had to haggle with him to get more points because the grading is that bad. The tests are writing psuedocode on paper which makes it very hard to not be able to mess up a little thing since you can't test a piece of paper.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP4600 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: nice guy whom u can talk to but very sneaky.he tells you to go to his office hours but he isnt there half the time.does a review but doesnt answer most questions.His grading is horrible and you end up negotiating for points.when he does any kind of reviews, ask questions and know the answer exactly the way he words them or else...

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP2500 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Well, this guy's class pretty much drove me nuts last semester. His notes stunk, he would SOMETIMES let you copy the stuff totally before removing it from the screen, and his ability to articulate is terrible. HOWEVER, he's very nice, and if you have questions, MAKE SURE you see him during office hours and ask. Woulda boosted my grade a lot.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP4600 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: This professor is tricky. Always when he said something was going to be easy or straight forward, it was exactly the opposite. He does not curve and design his test for students not to get a 100 wether for complexity or due to the length of the text. My GPA is 3.515 and I always try my best to get an A in comp major classes. This time, I got a B.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP2500 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: This is a challenging class.  The first couple of weeks I thought I was going to for sure fail. However, if you attend class the material will gradually make sense.  Also, he will be very helpful if you go to his office hours with any questions.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP4600 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: First of all, had about 10 people retaking class this semester. Assignments are hard but doable, little help from TA for Obj 1 & 2. Tests are hard, reviews don't cover 100%. Final was a total surprise - had about 3 questions not covered during review. Some questions, wording were hard to understand. Good Luck!

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP4020 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: 2nd semester I had him and this one was better.  Hated when he randomly called on people for questions, leaving u feeling dumb if you didn't know.  But the tests weren't hard.  Not many defns.  Mostly memorizing coding he gave & example problems.  Only 3 programming assignments which weren't hard. Used only the 1st 2 chapters of the book. Got an A!

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: This man obviously knows the material but he is a horrible teacher. His lectures are vague and imprecise, his guidelines and expectations are never stated and he refuses to clarify them, and he never writes actual code, just error-filled pseudocode. On top of this, he's a very harsh grader. No one I spoke to earned an A in his class.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP4600 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Not really a fair teacher.    If you are taking this class, make sure you are anything but a CS major.   He expects you do know more so he grades the tests harder.  (I confronted him on this, and he basically told me that was the way it was).   I ended up passing but only because my project was prefect.   Its a shame you have no choice to take him.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: His accent is OK, don't buy the book it's pointless,make sure to go to his reviews for the tests, will take points off on questions out of nowhere,labs were pointless(normally lasted 5-10 minutes..Thanks for wasting my gas TAs) you have to go because they are 10% of your grade. Overall not a great teacher but I wouldn't be upset if I had him again

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: EEL4882 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Teaches somewhat well, but reviews for tests are absolutely useless and only cover about a third of what is actually on the tests. Make sure you go to class because he sometimes makes announcements one time only (especially regarding the IT project). Programs aren't too bad, but it's better to use the TAs for help rather than the professor.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Decent teacher. You get used to his accent. Projects are not difficult but they're not the kind you can write in couple of hours. Do yourself a favor and at least read what the project should accomplish few days prior due date. The tests are kind of hard, mainly definitions and some code/grammar stuff. They are covered well on the review day.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP4600 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: This Prof needs to understand that some of his courses are composed of people from different majors and not all of them are at the same level. He treat I.T, C.S and EEL the same which is pretty unfair. besides that he has a thick accent & test are pretty hard.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP4600 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: (merged course w/ EEL4882).  He teaches by asking questions and having people guess until someone gets it right, which sucks, but the notes were decent.  His test reviews pretty much ARE the test, so know them well and memorize his EXACT wording for definitions. Course project is hard but doable. no book use, no quizzes, doesn't take attendance.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP2500 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Follow up with and study the notes, or else you'll be lost. Tests are incredibly specific and you can lose points for improper wording in definitions, but not impossible to pass. Lectures were tedious and we sometimes spent an entire class on one algorithm. The prof is nice, occassionally humorous, but sometimes difficult to understand.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP2500 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Very frustrating!!! IF WE DID ACTUAL CODE ON A COMPUTER IN CLASS INSTEAD OF ALL THAT PSUOCODE the class would be better.  Also the lab assistant is hell.  STUDY STUDY and STUDY SOME MORE  Also the book is NOT VERY HELPFUL. NO test questions come from it  just notes sometimes the same one MEMORIZE THE NOTES!!!!!

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3402 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: I had Dr. Montagne for Systems Software. He wasn't really a bad professor, and he had a quirky accent that was at times difficult to understand. He never seemed very interested in teaching, but he knew the material. Pro: he posts all of his powerpoint lectures online, and these are very helpful. Con: objectives were often not clear for projects.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: EEL4882 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: He took over this class and taught the IT/CpE/EE students COP 4600. His lectures can be boring and the OS project is very difficult if you don't have a solid grasp of C. Contact is also difficult. But the exams can be either very hard or very easy, since he practically tells exactly what will be on the exam.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP2500 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: I hate pseudo-code. Needs to teach javascript in the class or something more useful. Having programming experience is a plus but some algorithms are hard to remember. Especially matrices and linked lists.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP2500 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: An intro programming class yet he expected so much more from the group.  Luckily I knew programming and formed a study group to help them pass. HORRIBLE TEACHER.  And as for the ladies, beware, he flirts incessantly, but he will at least try to help you.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: EEL4882 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: He took over the class halfway through the semester when prof Lee left.  Reviews helped for the tests, but he grades tests pretty hard.  If you use an incorrect word, you lose a lot of your points. We had 3 of the hardest programming assignments all due in one month(we were combined with COP4600). You can do okay, but you have to work hard for it!

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP2500 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Tough to understand.  His three exams are 10 questions, mixed definitions and pseudocode algorithms.  Hard to figure out what he's looking for if he marks something wrong.  Will notice if you don't go to class.  Labs are very easy HTML and Javascript problems.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP4020 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: He is a pretty good teacher.  It seems you understand the concepts right after the test.  When he reviews for the test he gives away about 80% of the test exactly.  Assignments were easy, and not bad.  He seemed nice and helpful if you just went and talked to him.  The reasons he takes points off test answers is sometimes bizarre.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP4020 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: Not very clear explaining concepts. Makes things more difficult than they need to be. He's a nice guy and will go out of his way to make sure you understand the material if you ask questions though (particularly in office hours). Tells you almost exactly what will be on the test. Easy assignments.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Textbook was worthless. He's hard to understand sometimes, but you can easily convince him to make the tests open note/book, and suddenly the class is a breeze. The material you learn seems pretty worthless though. Take a different elective if you can, it's nothing like what you think it will be.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Do not buy the book! the book was useless. This class was pretty hard but he will help you through the material. Most of the tests including the final was openbook open notes.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP4020CDA4150 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Not helpful, felt like I was inconveniencing him during office hours. He was teaching 5 courses at the time. He crammed assignments in at the end of the semester with little/no explanation. Tests were covered unimportant topics, while main concepts were overlooked. Frequently referred to other courses I had not taken, nor were they prerequisites.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CDA4150 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: I have also been insulted by him during his office hours (it seems like everyone but the females have been).  A lot of the phrasing on his test questions are subjective (due to his limited English) and he will not respond to any students who have difficulty with his horrible style of teaching.  Great lecturer, but a horrible teacher.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CDA4150 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: For one, he can be very rude and uncaring in his office hours.  Doesn't email back.  He gives assignments using Verilog, but doesn't know how to use it himself. Shows favoritism to females. Seems to have a english lang problem, which sucks when he asks for paragraph answers on tests and nit-picks your wording (when his own is bad).
Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CDA4150 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Gave homework that he didn't explain

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CDA4150 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Very good professor, very hard class but you learn a lot.  Projects are impossible, even with help from the TA and a partner.  Expect to study, but you really do learn the material.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CDA4150 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: B+ | Review: Impossible tests. Impossible assignments. Great professor, he knows his stuff, but he doesn't come down to your level. Don't ask him anything about the assignment, he doesn't know anything about it. Pray that you have a english speaking TA. He's a great teacher, but talks way too fast, and hard to understand. Prepare to work your ass off to pass.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CDA4150 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Very happy and excited about teaching.  Was very rude first couple of times during office hours but he shined up to me after I just wouldn't go away.  Tests can be from left field and dont really cover the material he teaches.  His assignments are gruelingly long and he expects them done ON TIME and complete.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CDA4150 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: I can see how there are mixed feelings.  I however am fed up with this class.  I too have been insulted while speaking with him.  His vocabulary is limited and his grammer is worse.  He brings no materials to lecture and spends a lot of time drawing.  The limited lecture, and lack of materials, leave you unprepared to study for the diffictult tests

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CDA4150 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: He puts his effort alot on the class. He is  very nice and great prof in UCF of CS dep. trying to help students as possible. He always smiles and speaks softly. Wonderful!
Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: 5XXX | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: No Comments

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3269 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: This professor is the worst I've ever had in 6 years of college. I've had my problems, but this professor is a flat out liar.  He insulted me in his office, and kicked me out once so that he could give attention to a girl he appeared to be flirting with. This is typical behavior that can be observed

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP4600 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Hard tests, where you can't really predict what is going to be on test. Project are  hard like test. There is large discrepancy between knowledge  he transfers onto students and what he expect to receive back on test and projects. He is good when it comes to helping ouside class (visit him as often

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COT4210 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: Level of class is right.  He reiterates a lot so concepts stick in your mind.  Projects are challenging but doable.  Time for projects is fine.  Garding is slow.  Class notes could improve.  Exams are just right.  Go to his office at least once.  Grading is OK.  Good teacher and fun class.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: This guy is GREAT, knows the materal well, if you don't understand something he will present it in a different way.  Test are baised off the HW but U have to do the homework, it is graded.  Very easy to do though.  I will take him again in another class.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP5611 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Very keen and interested in students actually learning something. Has a tendency to be overzealous, and cause a lot of pain, but if you show what you learned (visit to his office regularly) and be open regarding your problems etc. you will fair very well.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: He is very helpful!!! Go to his office if you got any problem. He has a nice attitude towards students. Little bit accent though....
Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CDA4150 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: No Comments
Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: Soft spoken, spanish accent - tests from homework - knows what he's teaching - nice guy!

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Good Teacher, Tests from Homework. But he picks the tests apart. Good at explaining difficult concepts. Speeks Decient English. Does Not Pop Quiz. Overall Good!

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: Not_Sure_Yet | Review: An alright teacher. Material is not that difficult to learn. Very helpful if you don't understand the material. Doesn't speak english that well. He's a somewhat a fair teacher. I would recommend him just for this class.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Review: This is the guy to take.  He test are almost the same as the HW and he it always willing to help if you go to his office.  Notes in class can get into detail but doesnt test off too much.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CDA4150 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: Computer Arch teacher - kinda soft spoken and a little hard to understand.  Very helpful, exams and projects are challenging.  Makes many mistakes when lecturing.  Extends deadlines at the last minute on projects.

Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: CGS3269 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Not so bad, just have to do homework, which is similar to tests, see him in office for help--is very willing to help.  Kind of unclear in class, but will explain more in office.
Professor: Euripides Montagne | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: I HATE HIM ... I HATE HIM

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A | Review: She's very friendly and explains things slowly (sometimes too slowly) for students. The class is very easy so long as you do the work, tests are accurate to what is taught as well. I hardly attended lectures but had no problem using the resources she provides to catch up. Great teacher and format. Grading is fair.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: C+ | Review: I personally did not enjoy professor Angell's class. She goes through the material very fast and it is hard to keep up. She also doesn't explain some concepts thoroughly. The midterm and final can each be taken twice so that is fine overall. The homework is hard and a simple extra letter can get you 25 points deducted, so that is a downside.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A | Review: I LOVED THIS CLASS. She made coding so enjoyable for me that I changed my major to Computer Engineering. She is an absolutely amazing professor. The class is entirely just lectures with weekly assignments and labs. Nothing hard at all. Two attempts on the one midterm and final. But you probably won't even need it. Super easy and super fun. Enjoy!!!
Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A | Review: Fantastic lecturer and professor. Honestly a great class, I recommend highly.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: She is super nice and fun. Not mandatory attendance and posts lecture that are easy to follow. The midterm and final are easy and not hard if you stopped going to class. She is very nice and gives extra credit.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: CDA3103C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: Incomplete | Review: Sarah was a delight. She was incredibly helpful, especially with reviewing the exams. She WILL help you out if you seek it. This class is not easy though. The exams were very difficult for me. However, she knew what she was doing and even gave a bunch of credit back for showing that you knew what you were doing. This is what a professor should be.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: CDA3103C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: Amazing professor. Deserves higher rating. Brings joy to the classroom, teaching this class for years. Class gets difficult as you go through but if you pay attention in lectures you will do just fine! Two quizzes on same topic in two weeks, and took whichever score was higher. Make sure to go to test corrections!

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: B+ | Review: Professor Angell is very nice. Attendance is not mandatory, but all course information is easily accessible on Canvas. However, some TAs may not grade accurately, so it is advisable to reach out to the professor if you encounter any issues. She typically responds quickly. Be sure to complete your homework honestly and thoroughly grasp the material.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: CDA3103C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: Great professor, very funny and engaging. She always made class enjoyable, and if you couldn't make it to lecture all the resources were online with study sets. Weekly quizzes count towards a considerable portion of your grade, but you get multiple attempts and the questions aren't too difficult. She also gives a very helpful sample midterm/final.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: CDA3103C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: Great and caring professor. Test are very reasonable and there is a lot of opportunities to practice. Project can be difficult but you can work on it in groups!

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: Sarah is a really caring professor, she explains things pretty simply and has a smile on her face. You will still need to study, watch youtube videos, etc. but it's not rocket science. The thing that helped me the most was going to the TAs during their office hours, shoutout Betty Elliott! Helped a lot to complete the weekly coding assignments.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: CDA3103C | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: B+ | Review: I'm so glad I took this course by Prof. Angell over Montagne. Made my semester a breeze, and the material is not that hard to understand. The only complaint is the MIPS Datapath module, which you have a project with a group of 3, mid-semester. Overall, the homework is very simple, with weekly short quizzes and many makeup opportunities. Solid 4/5.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: She is a great teacher the only issue for me was that she went really fast through the content in her lectures. The tests however are really easy and she does a great job with helping you prepare for it.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A- | Review: Good professor, the class is not very difficult even with no prior coding expereince. You will have homework every weekend that takes some time.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A | Review: Very friendly for beginners, I would say. Projects aren't rocket science and the lectures are straightforward. She's got an awkward sense of humor, but for what it's worth, she's a good teacher. Would recommend if starting makes you nervous.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: Could go to any of the lectures (don't have to go to your own section), which is really nice. Preferred Professor Dencker's (the other professor for COP 2500) lectures. Professor Angell gave more examples, whereas Dencker explained them a little more but gave fewer examples.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A- | Review: You can know absolutely nothing about coding and still pass this class as long as you show up to the lectures (even though they're not mandatory), go to the labs (also mandatory, but it helped me understand difficult concepts), and take/review notes before doing any tests. Very nice teacher, great at what she does.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: This was the best professor I've had so far. She communicates CS concepts really well in simple terms for someone like me, who was going into coding completely blind, to understand easily. Tests and quizzes are all directly from lectures and practice problems. Really nice experience.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: CDA3103C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: Had her for COP2500, enjoyed it and got an A. Currently taking her for CDA3103 and have an A, she's pretty easygoing. Attendance not required, everything you need's online. No unnecessary hw assignments, just weekly quizzes (half of which are optional). Big project near end of semester.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: CDA3103 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: She's goated. The class itself is hard but she makes it very clear and easy to understand if you follow her lectures.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: Professor Angell is AMAZING. This material for this course is so accessible, and she's excellent at teaching it. While attendance isn't mandatory, GO TO CLASS. You won't regret it if you're here to learn.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: Angell literally lives up to the name. She is the sweetest professor and her lectures are easily understandable. If you fail this class, it's on you. TAKE HER, SHES AMAZING!!!!

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: CDA3103C | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: B | Review: The professor is very smart and very chill. The class itself is kind of boring (unless you are really interested in computer hardware / assembly). There is one project that you can do alone or with 2 friends, and she gives you enough time to finish it, so make sure to start early. Aside from that, chill class and chill professor.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: CDA3103C | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: Prof Angell is a good professor. Lectures are boring, she does seem to know a lot but its hard to keep your attention on such a boring topic sometimes. Quizzes are all online due Friday. You have two attempts throughout two weeks. Class grade depends on how you do on midterm and final and project. Guaranteed a B if you do bare minimum

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: The class itself is designed to be easy, and she is willing to explain concepts to any level of understanding / answer simple questions during lecture without losing patience. The grade is mostly programs, as well as lab assignments which I found easier to do outside of the lab.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: B+ | Review: Ms. Angell was amazing in her lectures. Very positive and happy to slow down and help explain something if needed. Weekly assignments which include one coding program and for the lab a few smaller assignments. If you go to the lab they just give you the answers to the weekly smaller assignments in there. Exams are easy with 2 attempts each.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: B | Review: Her voice is so gentle. She goes over the lectures pretty quickly in my opinion, but she will stop to see if you have questions. I've had to go home & look over the lesson because she was going so fast with the examples, it was hard to keep up w/. There's a midterm & a final, The good thing is she lets you take those twice & keeps the higher score.
Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: CDA3103C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: B | Review: Extremely kind and knowledgeable about the subject.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: CDA3103C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: i didn't really go to class, so i can't speak on the lectures. i found the material really interesting though. the majority of the grade is the final/midterm/project, and the rest of the grade were weekly online quizzes. there were no other grades, but if you do bad on the final/midterm you can go to office hours for partial credit on them.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: CDA3103 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: While I did find the content in this class kind of boring, I believe Angell did a great job of teaching it and brought good energy to lectures. This class is mostly graded on the midterm, final and a programming project all of which are very easy if you pay attention in lecture and go through the study sets each week.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: CDA3103C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: B | Review: The class is very manageable and can be passed without attending a single lecture. Her study sets are very good for all the assignments and tests, and will get you a good grade if you actually use them. She's very kind and reasonable during score clarification (a way to get lost points from tests). Just remember to study for the final properly!

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: B | Review: Easy class if you go to the lectures. The lectures are taught well. Programs make up most your grade, and there are harsh grading TA's which dumped my grade.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: Took it for the GEP req, didn't know anything about CS. Professor Angell codes the programs in class step by step and explains everything well. Two tests, midterm and final, both extremely similar to sample exams given. Weekly hw programs were hardest. She posts all notes and in-class work but it's better to attend as many lectures as possible.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: CDA3103C | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Professor Angell was overall good, but the class itself is completely boring. I felt like I could not pay attention for more than 10 minutes, and when I came back to the class I had already lost so much of the content. She just presents the slides, so it is HARD to VISUALIZE what is going on. My experience was bad overall, but she wants to help us.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: B | Review: Very funny and knowledgeable professor. She coded all of the programs step by step with the class and explained everything as simple as possible. If you go to every lecture and stay on top of your work you should get an A.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: Grading definitely takes a long time to go through, but without going to lecture due to exterior circumstances I am looking at a probably getting a B right now. You can attend any lab section, not just the one you signed up for. You just have 1 program (assignment) a week and they can be tedious or more than it needs to be with assignment criteria.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: Incomplete | Review: Currently sitting pretty at an A because of Ms.Angell's amazing teaching skills. She makes concepts easy to understand and makes her classes fun to be in. Unless you completely skip out on class there's really no reason you should struggle. If you do, however, she's always willing to go through concepts during her office hours or even in class.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: Professor Angell was an amazing instructor for COP2500C. It is a newer course, but she does a great job introducing the concepts of Python. Homework and lab assignments are engaging, which really helps understanding of the material. If she instructed some higher level CS courses, I would not hesitate to pick a class instructed by her.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: Very nice and caring professor. Very good in office hours and doesn't expect too much work from the students. Easy class, she tries to help you out in a lot of ways with extra credit and other things.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: CDA3103C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: She lectures slow but that gives me a lot of time to process and take notes so I like that. I find the class confusing but she gives a lot of optional pactice that is very helpful. The labs are great prep for her exam. We get bascially two attempts for each quiz so very lenient grading imo. Lots of office hours.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: CDA3103 | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A | Review: The material was very confusing and she mostly just read off the slides for the entire class. Kind and willing to help but unfortunately the lectures were just nowhere near up to par.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: I went into this class with zero coding experience, but Prof Angell made it incredibly easy to understand. Her lecture style is easy to digest as well as generally very fun. The class has weekly assignments and labs, but both are easy and the labs don't require in-person attendance. You also get two attempts on the midterm and final. Overall great!

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2332 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: I loved Professor Angell's class! Her lectures aren't boring because she is always sharing new code for each lecture class. We had coding assignments due each Sunday and lab assignments are due on Sunday too. Depending on your lab instructor, labs are super helpful. I went to every lecture and did every assignment and ended with a 96!

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A- | Review: The professor taught in lectures mostly through examples, and there were weekly labs and lab assignments. My only complaint is sometimes homework assignments would be a little hard to figure out as some concepts weren't covered in depth in class beforehand.
Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: CDA3103 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: Confusing lectures but fair quizzes (open-note, 2 tries), midterm, and final.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: C | Review: This professor starts out welcoming and simple. You learn the basics for the first few weeks and its easy to follow but after the midterm passes, the concepts get difficult. During lecture she codes at her pace, hard to follow if you aren't typing at least 80 wpm. The slides could have a bit more explanation as well.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: This class was pretty good it was easy to understand and she allows another attempt for the midterm and final + posts reviews almost identical to the exam. Her office hours are also helpful because she'll trace the problem with you. Lots of opportunities to have an A. Would recommend :)

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: CDA3103 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: Class can seem pretty overwhelming at times but the midterm and final are pretty easy if you review the sample midterm and final she provides. One project (15% of grade) can be a little difficult but you can work in a group of 3.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: Sweet professor who works well with students during office hours. Lectures are fun, and she engages the students. The assignments get moderately difficult at the end. She likes to talk to students before and after lectures. 45% of the class is exams, and she has extra credit as well. Slower grader & you can challenge scores sometimes.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: C+ | Review: professor was the best, the TA's were not though- also they grade super harshly?? It's a beginner python class-

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: CDA3103C | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: Graded basically on quizzes you take weekly. They are very similar to the practice sets she publishes, and u have 2 tries. One group project towards the end of the semester, also a midterm and final. She allows you to show up at her office after the midterm + final to get more points based on your scratch paper so dont miss that! Overall good class

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: CDA3103C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: She is an excellent professor. The course includes weekly online quizzes (35%). Each quiz topic spans two weeks, and the higher score is kept (pretty easy). In addition a group coding project (15%), a midterm (25%), and a final exam (25%). She is very accommodating if you provide proper documentation for any issues. Overall, pretty easy class.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: CDA3103C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: Very caring professor who always tries to help when she can. The class is pretty much graded by module quizzes(35%), a project(15%), a midterm(25%), and a final(25%), so be careful not to mess up too much or it will reflect in your grade. The content felt outdated and dragged at times, esp when learning about MIPS... but she makes it enjoyable.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: The class is easy as expected for an introductory programming class. Her lectures are definitely worth attending if you want to keep up with the material as she does a good job explaining every topic. Labs and assignments are very reasonable but it's easy to lose points for small mistakes. Just beware that grading takes a long time for assignments.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: B | Review: The class is great for an introductory programming class, and Angell is great at explaining topics. I only struggled in the class because I didn't take it as seriously as I should have. As long as you pay attention in class and don't procrastinate, especially for the last couple assignments, you should do well.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: She provides plenty of examples for newbie coders and the programs help you think critically. I thought this class would be a joke, but it can be quite difficult. Thankfully the assignments and labs provided a coherent view into concepts I will be using in the future of coding.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: She is a slow grader so beware and she is slow at responding to emails, the class itself wasn't too bad, if you do things last minute you might have a little trouble. The material itself was easy you don't have to show up to every class to understand it (don't skip every lecture though).

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: B- | Review: Engaging and very knowledgeable. She knows what she's teaching and is obviously passionate about it. Only reason I did not get a higher grade is because, unlike Prof. Angell, I am not excited about computer science. At least I took her class to make something I care little about just a bit more bearable.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: B | Review: This class is an intro to programming class that gives you a great hands on experience writing code for beginners. Professor Angell had amazing documentation with helpful TAs as well. Exams are proctored in person, so it is recommended you put in some effort. Tough grading on coding assignments, but I would take it again!

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: Prof. Angell is so awesome!! She is very kind and understanding. She makes topics easy and will thoroughly explain topics. There is 1 main assignment, and 0-3 lab assignments almost every week. They are easy, the lab is not req. to go to. You get 2 attempts at mid-term and final. The lectures are entertaining and very helpful.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: B+ | Review: Professor Angell teaches a clear, structured class, though grading can be particular. Engaging lectures and helpful TA support, but stay on top of assignments. Unstuck study was a great study tool alongside the practice tests.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP3502H | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: Probably the easiest CS1 professor based on what I've heard. Grading takes forever and assignments can be difficult, but she is very helpful during office hours; she will straight up give you the answers. Tests were reasonable and quizzes have 3 attempts. Gave lectures that were almost completely code-based; great at giving code demonstrations.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP3502H | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: She has good lectures/code notes and is very helpful during office hours if you need her. Program grading is stringent, and takes months. The TA doesn't put any notes in the rubric, so you don't know what you did wrong. 8 programming assignments, and 10? online quizzes (3 attempts.) The 3 exams are reasonable if you know what you are doing.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: Lectures were useless for me. Each week you have 2 lab quizzes with unlimited attempts and 2 programming assignments. The programs started off easy but got difficult and very time-consuming quickly. TAs take forever to grade the programs so you won't know your grade until the end, which is really annoying. Midterm and final were super easy.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A | Review: This was one of my all-time favorite classes. She was very good at explaining the concepts and encouraged students to ask questions. She would start with basic info from the slides but she mostly taught by hands-on coding which I loved. The course structure was very clear and easy to follow which I appreciated. She organized it by weeks.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A | Review: Very easy professor. Lectures leave no stone unturned. She let me resubmit an assignment with no penalty after a small mistake hurt my grade. You will have to put in quality practice outside of class to get the intuition for some of the concepts down (especially the later ones). Only complaint is that grades took a long time to get back.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 2 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: C | Review: Grading took forever in this class and codes had to be completed a very specific way or else you would get low scores on the hw and lab assignments. Midterm and final both had practice tests which were super helpful for reviewing. Overall if you don't have prior coding experience, this class can be challenging if computer science isn't your thing.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A- | Review: Coming in with very little coding experience, I found her class fairly easy. She spent lots of time in class exploring the "what ifs", which helped to develop a stronger understanding of Python and how it works. 2 assignments a week, but receiving the grades for them was several weeks behind. Lab attendance not needed but helpful when stuck.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: C | Review: You have to code exactly to what she wants. I learned coding outside of the class and tried to code my way and got a lot of point off of my assignments and didn't realize till it was too late because grading took a long time.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: B+ | Review: She's very good at explaining concepts and trying to energize the class at 8:30. Stickler when it comes to grading misplaced characters can cause you to lose valuable points on a project that's based out of 50. VERY late grader so you have to truly understand concepts/read rubrics/quadruple check work etc. to get an A.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A- | Review: Had to take her class twice bc I was not serious the first time, but she was so good I took the exact class again. She gives half-week grace periods on ALL hw and labs. Last class I let my lab grade slip, so beware of that. She sorta teaches like u already know computers, but if u use the resources she offers you'll do great!

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: A very beginner friendly programming class, although it can be tough to deal with the strict grading that comes with learning computer science so read the instructions very carefully. She gives out multiple projects a week but they're not tough. The lectures are fun and hands on since you follow her creating programs with input from the class.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP3502 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: Great lectures that were carefully planned. Homework could be a bit difficult but her office hours were always open to help you with anything you needed.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: Not sure yet | Review: This class is fairly easy especially if you have some experience with python. Her lectures provided good examples that really helped with the programming projects. The only major downside to this class is that it takes super long for the grades to be put in (multiple programs not graded until finals week).

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: CDA3103C | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: B | Review: Good teacher but kind of slow for me. Made the class feel way more boring than it should've. Quizzes every week that are pretty straightforward and one project due at the end of the semester that is about the entire class. Overall not that difficult. Tests were a little tricky but the reviews were helpful.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: B+ | Review: Loved this class! Her lectures were easy to follow and taught me a lot, even though often they were boring. I like how she teaches while coding, so as long as you follow her on your own you will learn a lot. I enjoyed the homework assignments and the labs, the instructions were very clear. The homework does get more difficult with time.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: CDA3103C | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: Two quizzes every module, one with highest score counted. Material isn't overwhelming at all, and she gives practice exams for each exam that basically reflect what she is going to put on the real exam. The group project can be a little overwhelming at first if you don't know c, but its not too difficult once you understand MIPS properly.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: B | Review: I'm not really a comp sci person, so this class was rough for me. While not mandatory attendance, go, because it will hurt in the long run if you don't. She's really passionate about her field which also makes her knowledgeable. She loves student participation as well and everything is on the Webcourses after class. Overall: Really kind and great

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: A | Review: The class can be a breeze if you attend lectures, as she does in-class examples which help understand the material. The weekly programs take 4hrs to do at most and utilize most of the skills that you're going to be tested on anyway. Content gets harder after the midterm and can be confusing if you don't attend the lectures, so attend the lectures!!

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: CDA3103 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: B+ | Review: Very caring professor, give you the study set and 2 attempts for each quiz. No homework. The teaching style wasn't the best tho.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: B | Review: I loved this professor! She explains everything in her lectures very clearly and if you don't understand she answers all questions during the lectures, through emails, or during her office hours. Her labs and lectures are not mandatory and all the information is on canvas. Her assignments are hard and time-consuming if you don't go to class though.
Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: D+ | Review: Doesn't go in depth with anything. TA's are very strict on grading. Just take Denecker

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: Honestly made the class very enjoyable. Found the lectures very useful even though they are not mandatory. Tests arent too difficult either as long as you know what you're doing. Lab is also not mandatory and not needed unless you're struggling.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: B | Review: Grades minimal and programs graded tough. Tests are taken in EPC and weighted heavy. Very challenging for an intro level class. Nice professor though.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: CDA3103 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: Goofy and kind professor. If you want a head start, review binary arithmetic, truth tables, and brush up on C. Lectures go more indepth if you're interested. If not, the quizzes and webcourses are more than enough to pass. The project isn't that bad; It requires bare minimum C pointer knowledge, but a good understanding of MIPS datapath.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: Sarah was super nice and patient, although she does tend to speed things up randomly so try to keep up. She's a great prof to take for those with no experience in coding, her lectures are pretty interesting. 3 labs + a program due every week are challenging yet doable, along with the midterm and final. Here's a tip: Make friends in your lecture!

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A- | Review: She's pretty easy if you've had past coding experience. She can ramble in lectures and go off-topic depending on student's questions but she always explains things when needed. Can type really fast and it can be hard to understand the code. But there are many notes and practice programs on Canvas to learn off of.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A- | Review: I love Professor Angell! She's very caring to those who are struggling like me. I struggled a lot because I was new to coding overall, but she's very compassionate and easygoing. My learning style doesn't match up with her teaching style unfortunately, so that also attributed to why I struggled to learn.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: CDA3103 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: She isn't the best at teaching, but she puts a lot of effort into organizing the web course, she is very forgiving with grades as she gives you 2 attempts for every quiz.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: CDA3103 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: She doesn't have a good teaching style, and it isn't easy to focus on the lecture. But she seems the best compared to other professors for this course at UCF.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2500C | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: Overall gives clear instructions each lecture, but seems to rely more on discussion classes and student time to get information across. An easy class with this professor if you have prior programming experience, a hard class if you don't. If you don't you WILL have to use your own time to study the matetial, or else you risk steadily falling behind

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP3223H | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: I appreciated how accessible the class was. Lectures were given in person, but you could also access them via zoom, and they were recorded so you could view them later if you missed class, and the code written during class was published so you could refresh yourself on what was covered during the lecture. She is also very fun and engaging.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP3223H | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: Great Professor. The RateMyProfessor score should definitely be higher than it is. Overall assignments were clear and thoroughly constructed. Lectures can be dull to some, but I enjoyed them overall. Great Professor. Would definitely recommend and I hope to take her again.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: CDA3103 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Review: Decent lecturer thought its better to just go through the study sets than to go to lectures. Midterm, project and final. Project can be difficult if you haven't taken CS1 but if you are with a group it should be fine. Module every 2 weeks with 2 attempts on a quiz for it. Going through the study sets allows you to get 100's on every quiz.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: CDA3103 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: This class is really boring. However, professor Angle did her best. Class is really organize, the final is really weird if you don't focus on 3 last chapter on this class

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: CDA3103 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: I had her for Comp Org and she was great. Every module has two small online quizzes and the lower score of the two is dropped. The lectures aren't always amazing but they do always get the job done. There is one big project at the end that took me a week to finish, but it was the only one and it wasn't much of an issue.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: CDA3103 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: In summary: this class is boring but easy. Lectures bored me as we flipped through powerpoints. Weekly quizzes are super lenient (two attempts basically) and easy. Tests are easy as well. The group project toward the end WILL take you for a ride though.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Review: Takes half off for basic coding errors. One time I put a different input method (still worked) and she said my code was wrong despite giving the same output.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: I came into this class with no knowledge of programming and the professor made the lectures clear and easy to follow. They are all prerecorded so you can follow along at your own pace. She held office hours 3 times a week and always responded to my messages within 24 hours. This made it easy to clear up stuff I was confused on.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: CDA3103 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 2 | Review: Webcourses modules had resources aligned perfectly with the quizzes and exams so its straightforward to study and do well on them. Fun to attend the Zoom recordings especially with cat cam at the start and end of class! Generally upbeat during lectures and wasn't too boring listening to compared to other professors. Project at the end was hard tho!

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: Professor Angell was amazing! Always answered any question I asked about a lesson, and helped clarify any concepts. There were 8 projects in total and 3 exams. Projects 1-5 were super simple, projects 6-8 were very difficult and time consuming. The exams were all easy, multiple choice and written code portions. Overall, amazing Professor.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: CDA3103 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: Nice professor but boring lectures. She just read off the powerpoints that barely had any information on them. Two quizzes per module - lowest one dropped. Easy to get A's if you follow the study sets and take notes on the past quizzes. One C programming project, but we were barely prepared for the way it was set up. Way different from CS1 projects

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP3223C | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: A- | Review: The TA's graded the work. You write 8 programs, 10 quizzes, 3 exams. The first 5 programs are easy if you watch the weekly videos she posts and take notes. After that, they get very challenging. The exams are weighted pretty heavily. Part are multiple-choice and part has small codes you must write. Quizzes are easy. Average class. Late feedback.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: CDA3103 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: Prof Angell is very kind and has supplemental videos and powerpoints to help you understand material. These are very helpful for the quizzes and tests, which is good since lectures are filled with confusing jargon. 2 quizzes for each unit, the lower one dropped. Angell can be confusing, but gets a 4 for all the supplemental materials she made.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP3103C | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: C | Review: Quizzes have 2 attempts and you can see what you got wrong on your fist. Very little homework. Most of the grade is based on final/midterm/project. She's very good with answering questions and giving extra help to students that need it during lectures and is forgiving with making up past grades.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2332 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 4 | Grade Received: C+ | Review: The worst experience I ever had since starting UCF. Her lectures are too brief, doesnt cover enough to understand her own assignments. Will not answer basic questions, TAs constantly absent and condescending. The department does not care, either already be an expert or dont bother. The contempt and apathy I felt was disheartening. Wasted time.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: CDA3103 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: A | Review: Prof Angell is kind, but lectures can get boring. Lots of information, but not necessarily hard. Weekly quizzes (she drops lowest quiz for each subject), MIPS project, and two exams. Her web course is amazing since she includes pre-recorded lectures and lots of helpful materials for quizzes. Oh and everything is open-notes
Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP3223CDA3103 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: F | Review: You maybe have a hard time if you are a beginner programmer.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP4331 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: F | Review: Lectures were boring and mostly involved reading off of slides and diagrams posted online. Your final grade is based off of a few quizzes and two exams (taken at the EPC, not in class) and a programming project.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2332 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 5 | Grade Received: C+ | Review: She did not grade my papers nor explain why even if it was submitted on time it would not be late. and some of the code she reported that my code was identical to others in 3 lines or more but she didn't provide any proof or anything to prove it. When writing code that has some basic lines of code, we can repeat the same between two completely diff
Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP2332 | Quality: 1 | Difficulty: 1 | Grade Received: D | Review: see don't give me the grade for my homework and don't give me why see don't give me a grade
Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: Love that she posts panopto videos with step by step coding. Learned a lot, got the A.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: CDA3103 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: Definitely not mean spirited, pretty nice and solid at lecturing. This class didn't blow me away or anything but I did learn a substantial amount and had no difficulty passing based on her lectures. I would definitely take her whenever possible, she feels like a safe bet for any class.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP3223 | Quality: 3 | Difficulty: 4 | Review: Lectures ranged from 15-45 min but were super boring, making it easy to procrastinate. Some of the hw projects were easy but others took up to 40 hours to code. This is a tough class if youre a first time programmer because you have to stay motivated and not fall behind. Two lowest quizzes are dropped. She also took forever to release grades

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: CDA3103 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A+ | Review: Im surprised she doesnt have a higher rating. If you did badly on a quiz, she lets you take a second one and replaces it with the bad grade if you did better on it. The group project is a little confusing but she answers ANY questions you have through email or after class. She doesnt want you to fail.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: COP3223CDA3103 | Quality: 5 | Difficulty: 2 | Grade Received: A | Review: I took Prof. Angell for 2 classes and enjoyed them both. She is funny and knowledgeable about the material. The project for CDA was somewhat confusing at first, though you are free to consult other classmates and your group mates for help. Although her slides/material is all put online, I highly recommend taking notes. Exams/quizzes were very fair.

Professor: Sarah Angell | Course: CDA3103 | Quality: 4 | Difficulty: 3 | Grade Received: B | Review: Not a hard class, but there's a very small margin of error since we were only graded on quizzes, tests, and a project that required a lot of programming experience which wasn't necessary for the rest of the class.
